contests.office_does: A U.S. representative writes and votes on federal law and spending, conducts oversight, and provides constituent services.
contests.race_blurb: Two-term incumbent Marie Gluesenkamp Perez faces eight challengers spanning institutional conservatism, progressive economics, civil liberties, anti-party reform, and business-focused platforms.

candidates.name: Marie Gluesenkamp Perez
candidates.summary: Two-term incumbent and auto-shop owner emphasizing trades, manufacturing, rural investment, supply-side housing, abortion rights, and anti-corporate reforms.
highlights: Supports cutting red tape to build housing [S1]
Opposes corporate PAC money and introduced a congressional stock-trading ban [S1]
Supports abortion rights and rural health and infrastructure [S1]

spending.basis: Supports rural health, infrastructure, school, road, forest, and farmland investment.

reform.basis: Rejects corporate PAC money and introduced stock-trading and congressional-pay legislation.

healthcare.basis: Supports cheaper generics and rural care within existing programs.

social.basis: Explicit abortion-rights support.

experience.basis: Two-term incumbent with committee and legislative record.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 12

candidates.name: Brent Hennrich
candidates.summary: Project manager supporting progressive taxes, health-care expansion, infrastructure and social investment, ICE constraints, reduced war spending, and PAC independence.
highlights: Supports higher taxes on wealthy people and rejects corporate PAC money [S1]
Supports universal-health movement and investment [S1][S2]
Opposes wars and ICE practices [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicit higher taxes on wealthy people.

spending.basis: Broad school, infrastructure, health, broadband, and social investment.

reform.basis: Rejects corporate PAC funding.

immigration.basis: Opposes documented ICE practices.

healthcare.basis: Supports movement toward universal care but mechanism is unspecified.

defense.basis: Explicit opposition to wars.

experience.basis: No elected record.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 12

candidates.name: John P. Roco
candidates.summary: Therapist and former professor emphasizing immigration enforcement, traditional social positions, and a public-works housing concept.
highlights: Supports removing immigrants he describes as criminals [S1]
Opposes DEI in the military and transgender women in women's sports [S1]

immigration.basis: Supports removal of immigrants characterized as criminals while retaining others.

social.basis: Explicit traditional positions on military DEI and transgender sports.

experience.basis: No elected federal record.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 13

candidates.name: John Saulie-Rohman
candidates.summary: Power-industry operator centered on anti-donor, anti-two-party, anti-intervention, fiscal, and corporate-power critiques.
highlights: Argues major parties serve donors and corporations [S1][S2]
Criticizes military intervention and national-debt growth [S1]

spending.basis: Criticizes national debt growth.

reform.basis: Structural anti-donor and anti-two-party platform.

defense.basis: Opposes military intervention.

experience.basis: No elected experience.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 13

candidates.name: Troy Rasband
candidates.summary: IT professional supporting a wealth tax, expanded nutrition aid, low-income advocacy, and more affordable health care.
highlights: Supports a wealth tax and expanded SNAP [S1][S2]
Supports low-income advocacy and more affordable health care [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicit wealth-tax support.

spending.basis: Supports expanded SNAP and low-income programs.

experience.basis: No elected record.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 14

candidates.name: John Braun
candidates.summary: State Senate Republican leader and retired Navy captain emphasizing tax and spending restraint, enforcement, defense, border security, and parental rights.
highlights: Supports lower taxes, reduced spending, law enforcement, and parental rights [S1]
Supports border security and national defense [S3]
Long state legislative and budget record [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: Explicit lower-tax platform.

spending.basis: Explicit reduced spending and waste-control platform.

safety.basis: Explicit law-enforcement priority.

immigration.basis: Explicit border-security priority.

social.basis: Supports parental rights.

defense.basis: National-defense support and retired Navy leadership.

experience.basis: Long legislative, budget, military, and business leadership record.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 14

candidates.name: Antony Barran
candidates.summary: Entrepreneur and oyster farmer emphasizing business, workforce, affordable energy, education, natural resources, and outsider pragmatism.
highlights: Supports local economies, workforce training, small businesses, and affordable energy [S1][S2]
Runs against political insiders [S1]

climate.basis: Prioritizes energy affordability with responsible-resource language.

experience.basis: Business outsider with no elected record beyond precinct service.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 15

candidates.name: Austin Braswell
candidates.summary: Navy veteran emphasizing civil liberties, police and ICE accountability, surveillance limits, PAC independence, lower war spending, and AI ownership protections.
highlights: Supports police accountability and keeping ICE out of Vancouver [S1][S2]
Rejects corporate PAC money and supports reducing war spending [S1]
Supports surveillance limits and AI ownership protections [S1]

safety.basis: Police-accountability emphasis leans away from enforcement-first.

reform.basis: Rejects corporate PAC money and supports federal transparency.

immigration.basis: Explicitly supports excluding ICE from Vancouver.

tech.basis: Supports protections against AI-related ownership loss and surveillance.

defense.basis: Explicitly supports reducing war spending.

experience.basis: No elected record.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 15

candidates.name: Lawrence Kellogg
candidates.summary: Retired pilot supporting lower taxes, border enforcement, business conditions, transparency, voter access, and rejection of unsupported fraud claims.
highlights: Criticizes high taxation and open borders [S1]
Supports voter access and congressional transparency [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicit criticism of high taxation.

immigration.basis: Explicit open-border criticism and enforcement posture.

reform.basis: Supports transparency and voter access while rejecting unsupported fraud claims.

experience.basis: No elected record.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 16

contests.office_does: The assessor values taxable real property and administers property-tax exemption programs but does not set levy rates.
contests.race_blurb: Four-term incumbent Peter Van Nortwick emphasizes appraisal credentials and operational continuity, while real-estate investor Tyler Thoune campaigns on valuation accuracy, appeals, and customer service.

candidates.name: Tyler Thoune
candidates.summary: A real-estate investor running as an outsider focused on market-grounded assessments, transparency, and taxpayer appeals.
highlights: Says assessments should track the local market and high-value property should not be under-assessed relative to ordinary homes [S1]
Promises transparency, accountability for errors, and improved customer service [S1]

experience.basis: Runs as a first-time office outsider against a four-term incumbent, relying on private real-estate experience.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 25

candidates.name: Peter Van Nortwick
candidates.summary: The four-term incumbent assessor, emphasizing certified appraisal experience, exemption access, technology, and a smaller workforce.
highlights: Has served as assessor since 2011 and is a certified general appraiser [S1]
Supports expanded senior exemptions and a state homestead exemption [S1]
Says the office uses fewer full-time employees than when he was first elected [S1]

spending.basis: Highlights technology and operating the office with fewer employees as evidence of lower-cost administration.

experience.basis: Four-term incumbent with certified appraisal credentials and direct residential, agricultural, and commercial valuation experience.

taxes.basis: Supports senior property-tax exemptions and a state homestead exemption, a clear but limited tax-relief direction.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 25

contests.office_does: The auditor is the county's chief financial officer and administers elections, licensing, recording, and related public services.
contests.race_blurb: Four candidates contrast direct auditor-office finance experience with elected legislative, county, and municipal management backgrounds.

candidates.name: Mitchell Kelly
candidates.summary: Deputy auditor and former assistant state auditor emphasizing nonpartisan administration, finance, accuracy, and transparency.
highlights: Only candidate with direct Clark County Auditor management experience [S1][S2]
Has 15 years in government finance [S1]

spending.basis: Emphasizes financial oversight and operational accuracy.

experience.basis: Direct deputy-auditor management and government-finance record.

contests.office_does: The clerk preserves Superior Court records and manages court-related deposits, disbursements, filing access, and administrative systems.

Reports improvements to restitution collection, real-time document access, and electronic filing [S1]
Emphasizes record integrity, open access, efficiency, and responsive service [S1]

spending.basis: Centers efficiency and operational systems rather than program expansion.

experience.basis: Has led the clerk's office since 2010 and cites a concrete 16-year administrative and technology record.

experience.basis: Brings legal and mediation experience but no documented clerk-office administration or elected record.

contests.office_does: The county council adopts policy and budgets and oversees county services, land use, infrastructure, and public health.

Cites work on mental-health licensure, prescription-history access, and professional-license portability [S1]
Presents a nonpartisan, consensus-oriented approach [S1]

contests.office_does: A U.S. representative writes and votes on federal law and spending, conducts oversight, and provides constituent services.
contests.race_blurb: The open-seat field spans experienced officeholders and outsider candidates with differences on taxes, immigration, energy, reform, social policy, technology, and defense.

candidates.name: Jacek "Jack" Kobiesa
candidates.summary: Engineer emphasizing capitalism, liberty, constitutional limits, science, and accountability.
highlights: Engineer emphasizing capitalism, liberty, constitutional limits, science, and accountability. [S1]

experience.basis: First-time outsider.

candidates.name: Amanda McKinney
candidates.summary: County commissioner emphasizing agriculture, affordable conventional energy, enforcement, gun rights, and fiscal responsibility.
highlights: County commissioner emphasizing agriculture, affordable conventional energy, enforcement, gun rights, and fiscal responsibility. [S1]

spending.basis: Fiscal-responsibility and fraud-prevention emphasis.

safety.basis: Explicit law-enforcement priority.

climate.basis: Dams, nuclear, fusion, LNG and affordability-first posture.

immigration.basis: Explicit border enforcement.

experience.basis: Six years as county commissioner and national county leadership.

candidates.name: John Duresky
candidates.summary: Veteran and federal technology manager supporting labor, infrastructure, current health programs moving universal, ethics reform, and public lands.
highlights: Veteran and federal technology manager supporting labor, infrastructure, current health programs moving universal, ethics reform, and public lands. [S1]

spending.basis: Infrastructure, broadband, rural hospitals and subsidies.

reform.basis: Stock ban, Citizens United reversal, ethics rules.

healthcare.basis: Current-system protections with eventual universal coverage.

experience.basis: Substantial federal management but no elected record.

candidates.name: John C. Hughs
candidates.summary: Farmer, lawyer, engineer, and small-business candidate emphasizing debt, tax breaks, stewardship, family, church, and property rights.
highlights: Farmer, lawyer, engineer, and small-business candidate emphasizing debt, tax breaks, stewardship, family, church, and property rights. [S1]

taxes.basis: Small-business tax breaks.

spending.basis: National-debt reduction.

experience.basis: No elected record.

candidates.name: Favian Valencia
candidates.summary: Civil-rights attorney emphasizing constitutional protections and independent, cross-partisan governance.
highlights: Civil-rights attorney emphasizing constitutional protections and independent, cross-partisan governance. [S1]

experience.basis: First-time candidate.

candidates.name: Jerrod Sessler
candidates.summary: Repeat America First candidate opposing tax-and-spend policy and emphasizing faith, family, constitutional government, and anti-establishment politics.
highlights: Repeat America First candidate opposing tax-and-spend policy and emphasizing faith, family, constitutional government, and anti-establishment politics. [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicit opposition to taxes and property taxes.

spending.basis: Explicit anti-spending platform.

social.basis: Faith-and-family traditional-values platform.

experience.basis: Repeat candidate and primary winner but no officeholding record.

candidates.name: Devin Poore
candidates.summary: Software engineer supporting structural reform, public investment, clean energy, dignified immigration, and reduced foreign wars.
highlights: Software engineer supporting structural reform, public investment, clean energy, dignified immigration, and reduced foreign wars. [S1]

spending.basis: Farm stabilization and workforce programs.

climate.basis: Decentralized clean energy and wildfire management.

reform.basis: Term limits, stock ban, finance reform, lobbying transparency.

immigration.basis: Dignified immigration policy.

defense.basis: Opposes costly wars and Gaza policy.

experience.basis: No elected record.

candidates.name: Ken Vaz
candidates.summary: Technology entrepreneur emphasizing budgets, public safety, affordable energy, jobs, and innovation with worker protections.
highlights: Technology entrepreneur emphasizing budgets, public safety, affordable energy, jobs, and innovation with worker protections. [S1]

spending.basis: Responsible-budgeting emphasis.

safety.basis: Public-safety priority without detailed mechanism.

climate.basis: Energy affordability emphasis.

tech.basis: Innovation-friendly AI posture with worker protections.

experience.basis: First-time candidate.

candidates.name: Zac Rossi
candidates.summary: Nuclear operator proposing spending caps, subsidy reductions, later eligibility, Medicare competition, and targeted tax increases.
highlights: Nuclear operator proposing spending caps, subsidy reductions, later eligibility, Medicare competition, and targeted tax increases. [S1]

taxes.basis: Combines top-bracket and payroll increases with other restraint.

spending.basis: Specific spending caps, subsidy reductions, and later eligibility.

healthcare.basis: More private Medicare competition within current system.

experience.basis: No elected record.

candidates.name: Elpidia Saavedra
candidates.summary: Mayor combining infrastructure, safety, health, housing, clean energy, immigration reform, parental rights, lower taxes, and donor independence.
highlights: Mayor combining infrastructure, safety, health, housing, clean energy, immigration reform, parental rights, lower taxes, and donor independence. [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicit lower-tax position.

spending.basis: Infrastructure and public-safety funding.

climate.basis: Renewable-energy and clean-water support.

reform.basis: Independence from large donors.

social.basis: Parental-rights and faith-family platform.

experience.basis: Mayor and council record.

candidates.name: Matt Boehnke
candidates.summary: State senator and veteran emphasizing lower taxes, less regulation, conventional energy, border enforcement, fentanyl interdiction, faith, and family.
highlights: State senator and veteran emphasizing lower taxes, less regulation, conventional energy, border enforcement, fentanyl interdiction, faith, and family. [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicit lower taxes.

safety.basis: Fentanyl interdiction and enforcement.

climate.basis: Hydropower, nuclear, dams and affordability.

immigration.basis: Border enforcement.

experience.basis: Eight years legislative and prior council record.

contests.office_does: A U.S. representative writes and votes on federal law and spending, conducts executive oversight, and provides constituent services.
contests.race_blurb: Freshman incumbent Michael Baumgartner faces eleven challengers spanning independent reform, Democratic health-care and affordability platforms, and candidates with military, diplomatic, legal, medical, or business experience.

candidates.name: Nate Powell
candidates.summary: Powell is a firefighter and Marine veteran advocating billionaire-tax enforcement, universal health access, labor protections, waste reduction, and independence from corporate parties.
highlights: Supports ending billionaire tax avoidance and reducing waste and debt [S1][S2]
Supports universal health-care access and labor protections [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: He explicitly supports ending billionaire tax avoidance.

spending.basis: He combines universal-service ambitions with waste and debt reduction.

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate without an elected record.

reform.basis: He emphasizes independence from corporate parties.

healthcare.basis: He supports universal health-care access, though the dossier does not specify a single-payer mechanism.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Carmela Conroy
candidates.summary: Conroy is a former diplomat and deputy prosecutor emphasizing current-system health funding, reproductive autonomy, and executive and anti-corruption accountability.
highlights: Supports rural hospitals, Medicare, and Medicaid [S1][S2]
Supports reproductive autonomy and anti-corruption enforcement [S1][S2]

experience.basis: She has substantial diplomatic and prosecutorial experience but no documented elected record.

reform.basis: She emphasizes executive accountability and anti-corruption enforcement.

healthcare.basis: She supports funding rural hospitals, Medicare, and Medicaid within the current system.

social.basis: She explicitly supports reproductive autonomy.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Matthew Hayes
candidates.summary: Hayes is a retired physician and Air Force veteran emphasizing lower health costs, rural institutions, forests, and clean water.
highlights: Supports lower health-care costs and rural hospitals [S1][S2]
Supports farmers, schools, forests, and clean water [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate despite extensive military and medical experience.

healthcare.basis: He emphasizes lower costs and rural-hospital support without proposing single-payer transformation.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Bajun R. Mavalwalla
candidates.summary: Mavalwalla is a retired military officer and union member advocating balanced budgets, trade, alliances, infrastructure, and cross-party governance.
highlights: Supports balanced budgets and free trade [S1][S2]
Supports military alliances, infrastructure, and broadband [S1][S2]

spending.basis: He explicitly supports balanced budgets while also backing infrastructure investment.

experience.basis: He has military and campaign experience but no documented elected record.

defense.basis: He supports maintaining U.S. military alliances.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Michael McGarr
candidates.summary: McGarr is a veteran and retired editor advocating restored federal capacity, congressional oversight, and bipartisan institutional governance.
highlights: Supports restoring federal agencies and congressional oversight [S1][S2]
Opposes shutdowns and supports bipartisan governance [S1][S2]

spending.basis: He supports restoring federal-agency capacity rather than reducing it.

experience.basis: He offers military and journalism experience but no elected record.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Kevin Fagan
candidates.summary: Fagan is a filed Democratic candidate whose reviewed dossier documents only general affordability and representation priorities.
highlights: Official statement emphasizes affordability and representation [S1]

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Michael Baumgartner
candidates.summary: Baumgartner is the freshman incumbent and a former state senator and county treasurer emphasizing dams, agriculture, water, constituent service, and cross-party work.
highlights: Has served in Congress, the state Senate, and as county treasurer [S1][S2]
Supports Snake River dams, agriculture, water resources, and congressional integrity [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He is the incumbent with substantial prior state and county elected service.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Kyle Usrey
candidates.summary: Usrey is a lawyer and former university president advocating single-payer major-medical coverage, limits on ICE misconduct, AI regulation, and institutional term limits.
highlights: Supports single-payer major-medical reform [S1][S2]
Supports civil liability for unlawful ICE conduct, AI regulation, and Supreme Court term limits [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He has extensive professional and institutional leadership but no documented elected record.

reform.basis: He supports executive limits and Supreme Court term limits.

immigration.basis: He supports civil liability for unlawful ICE conduct.

healthcare.basis: He explicitly supports single-payer major-medical reform.

tech.basis: He explicitly supports AI regulation.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Andrew Bartleson
candidates.summary: Bartleson is a first-time candidate advocating universal health and dental coverage, antitrust action, political disclosure, and AI controls.
highlights: Supports universal health and dental coverage [S1][S2]
Supports stock-trading disclosure, antitrust action, and AI privacy and price-fixing controls [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate without an elected record.

reform.basis: He supports stock-trading disclosure as a political-accountability reform.

tech.basis: He supports AI privacy and price-fixing controls.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Ann Marie Danimus
candidates.summary: Danimus is an independent small-business consultant emphasizing no PAC money, affordability, reproductive rights, balanced immigration policy, and addiction services.
highlights: Rejects PAC money and supports program restructuring [S1][S2]
Supports abortion rights and immigration with border protection [S1][S2]

experience.basis: She offers business and caregiving experience rather than elected office.

reform.basis: She explicitly rejects PAC money and runs independently.

immigration.basis: She combines support for immigration with border protection.

social.basis: She explicitly supports abortion rights.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Richard Freudenberg
candidates.summary: Freudenberg is a retired Air Force lawyer emphasizing health access, rural hospitals, affordability, legal accountability, and opposition to tax cuts concentrated on wealthy people.
highlights: Supports health-care access and rural hospitals [S1][S2]
Opposes tax cuts concentrated on millionaires and billionaires [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: He opposes tax cuts concentrated on millionaires and billionaires.

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate despite extensive military legal experience.

healthcare.basis: He supports access and rural hospitals without proposing single-payer transformation.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: David Womack
candidates.summary: Womack is a retired military medical commander and rural-hospital executive advocating universal coverage, reproductive autonomy, climate and water research, and humane border security.
highlights: Supports universal coverage and sustainable rural hospitals [S1][S2]
Supports reproductive autonomy, climate research, water stewardship, and humane border security [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He has senior military and hospital experience but no elected record.

immigration.basis: His humane-border-security position combines enforcement and protections.

social.basis: He explicitly supports reproductive autonomy.

defense.basis: He supports defense while opposing forever wars.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

contests.office_does: A U.S. representative writes and votes on federal law and spending, conducts oversight, and provides constituent services.
contests.race_blurb: The open-seat field spans experienced officeholders and outsider candidates with differences on taxes, immigration, energy, reform, social policy, technology, and defense.

candidates.name: Jacek "Jack" Kobiesa
candidates.summary: Engineer emphasizing capitalism, liberty, constitutional limits, science, and accountability.
highlights: Engineer emphasizing capitalism, liberty, constitutional limits, science, and accountability. [S1]

experience.basis: First-time outsider.

candidates.name: Amanda McKinney
candidates.summary: County commissioner emphasizing agriculture, affordable conventional energy, enforcement, gun rights, and fiscal responsibility.
highlights: County commissioner emphasizing agriculture, affordable conventional energy, enforcement, gun rights, and fiscal responsibility. [S1]

spending.basis: Fiscal-responsibility and fraud-prevention emphasis.

safety.basis: Explicit law-enforcement priority.

climate.basis: Dams, nuclear, fusion, LNG and affordability-first posture.

immigration.basis: Explicit border enforcement.

experience.basis: Six years as county commissioner and national county leadership.

candidates.name: John Duresky
candidates.summary: Veteran and federal technology manager supporting labor, infrastructure, current health programs moving universal, ethics reform, and public lands.
highlights: Veteran and federal technology manager supporting labor, infrastructure, current health programs moving universal, ethics reform, and public lands. [S1]

spending.basis: Infrastructure, broadband, rural hospitals and subsidies.

reform.basis: Stock ban, Citizens United reversal, ethics rules.

healthcare.basis: Current-system protections with eventual universal coverage.

experience.basis: Substantial federal management but no elected record.

candidates.name: John C. Hughs
candidates.summary: Farmer, lawyer, engineer, and small-business candidate emphasizing debt, tax breaks, stewardship, family, church, and property rights.
highlights: Farmer, lawyer, engineer, and small-business candidate emphasizing debt, tax breaks, stewardship, family, church, and property rights. [S1]

taxes.basis: Small-business tax breaks.

spending.basis: National-debt reduction.

experience.basis: No elected record.

candidates.name: Favian Valencia
candidates.summary: Civil-rights attorney emphasizing constitutional protections and independent, cross-partisan governance.
highlights: Civil-rights attorney emphasizing constitutional protections and independent, cross-partisan governance. [S1]

experience.basis: First-time candidate.

candidates.name: Jerrod Sessler
candidates.summary: Repeat America First candidate opposing tax-and-spend policy and emphasizing faith, family, constitutional government, and anti-establishment politics.
highlights: Repeat America First candidate opposing tax-and-spend policy and emphasizing faith, family, constitutional government, and anti-establishment politics. [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicit opposition to taxes and property taxes.

spending.basis: Explicit anti-spending platform.

social.basis: Faith-and-family traditional-values platform.

experience.basis: Repeat candidate and primary winner but no officeholding record.

candidates.name: Devin Poore
candidates.summary: Software engineer supporting structural reform, public investment, clean energy, dignified immigration, and reduced foreign wars.
highlights: Software engineer supporting structural reform, public investment, clean energy, dignified immigration, and reduced foreign wars. [S1]

spending.basis: Farm stabilization and workforce programs.

climate.basis: Decentralized clean energy and wildfire management.

reform.basis: Term limits, stock ban, finance reform, lobbying transparency.

immigration.basis: Dignified immigration policy.

defense.basis: Opposes costly wars and Gaza policy.

experience.basis: No elected record.

candidates.name: Ken Vaz
candidates.summary: Technology entrepreneur emphasizing budgets, public safety, affordable energy, jobs, and innovation with worker protections.
highlights: Technology entrepreneur emphasizing budgets, public safety, affordable energy, jobs, and innovation with worker protections. [S1]

spending.basis: Responsible-budgeting emphasis.

safety.basis: Public-safety priority without detailed mechanism.

climate.basis: Energy affordability emphasis.

tech.basis: Innovation-friendly AI posture with worker protections.

experience.basis: First-time candidate.

candidates.name: Zac Rossi
candidates.summary: Nuclear operator proposing spending caps, subsidy reductions, later eligibility, Medicare competition, and targeted tax increases.
highlights: Nuclear operator proposing spending caps, subsidy reductions, later eligibility, Medicare competition, and targeted tax increases. [S1]

taxes.basis: Combines top-bracket and payroll increases with other restraint.

spending.basis: Specific spending caps, subsidy reductions, and later eligibility.

healthcare.basis: More private Medicare competition within current system.

experience.basis: No elected record.

candidates.name: Elpidia Saavedra
candidates.summary: Mayor combining infrastructure, safety, health, housing, clean energy, immigration reform, parental rights, lower taxes, and donor independence.
highlights: Mayor combining infrastructure, safety, health, housing, clean energy, immigration reform, parental rights, lower taxes, and donor independence. [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicit lower-tax position.

spending.basis: Infrastructure and public-safety funding.

climate.basis: Renewable-energy and clean-water support.

reform.basis: Independence from large donors.

social.basis: Parental-rights and faith-family platform.

experience.basis: Mayor and council record.

candidates.name: Matt Boehnke
candidates.summary: State senator and veteran emphasizing lower taxes, less regulation, conventional energy, border enforcement, fentanyl interdiction, faith, and family.
highlights: State senator and veteran emphasizing lower taxes, less regulation, conventional energy, border enforcement, fentanyl interdiction, faith, and family. [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicit lower taxes.

safety.basis: Fentanyl interdiction and enforcement.

climate.basis: Hydropower, nuclear, dams and affordability.

immigration.basis: Border enforcement.

experience.basis: Eight years legislative and prior council record.

contests.office_does: A U.S. representative writes and votes on federal law and spending, conducts executive oversight, and provides constituent services.
contests.race_blurb: Freshman incumbent Michael Baumgartner faces eleven challengers spanning independent reform, Democratic health-care and affordability platforms, and candidates with military, diplomatic, legal, medical, or business experience.

candidates.name: Nate Powell
candidates.summary: Powell is a firefighter and Marine veteran advocating billionaire-tax enforcement, universal health access, labor protections, waste reduction, and independence from corporate parties.
highlights: Supports ending billionaire tax avoidance and reducing waste and debt [S1][S2]
Supports universal health-care access and labor protections [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: He explicitly supports ending billionaire tax avoidance.

spending.basis: He combines universal-service ambitions with waste and debt reduction.

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate without an elected record.

reform.basis: He emphasizes independence from corporate parties.

healthcare.basis: He supports universal health-care access, though the dossier does not specify a single-payer mechanism.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Carmela Conroy
candidates.summary: Conroy is a former diplomat and deputy prosecutor emphasizing current-system health funding, reproductive autonomy, and executive and anti-corruption accountability.
highlights: Supports rural hospitals, Medicare, and Medicaid [S1][S2]
Supports reproductive autonomy and anti-corruption enforcement [S1][S2]

experience.basis: She has substantial diplomatic and prosecutorial experience but no documented elected record.

reform.basis: She emphasizes executive accountability and anti-corruption enforcement.

healthcare.basis: She supports funding rural hospitals, Medicare, and Medicaid within the current system.

social.basis: She explicitly supports reproductive autonomy.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Matthew Hayes
candidates.summary: Hayes is a retired physician and Air Force veteran emphasizing lower health costs, rural institutions, forests, and clean water.
highlights: Supports lower health-care costs and rural hospitals [S1][S2]
Supports farmers, schools, forests, and clean water [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate despite extensive military and medical experience.

healthcare.basis: He emphasizes lower costs and rural-hospital support without proposing single-payer transformation.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Bajun R. Mavalwalla
candidates.summary: Mavalwalla is a retired military officer and union member advocating balanced budgets, trade, alliances, infrastructure, and cross-party governance.
highlights: Supports balanced budgets and free trade [S1][S2]
Supports military alliances, infrastructure, and broadband [S1][S2]

spending.basis: He explicitly supports balanced budgets while also backing infrastructure investment.

experience.basis: He has military and campaign experience but no documented elected record.

defense.basis: He supports maintaining U.S. military alliances.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Michael McGarr
candidates.summary: McGarr is a veteran and retired editor advocating restored federal capacity, congressional oversight, and bipartisan institutional governance.
highlights: Supports restoring federal agencies and congressional oversight [S1][S2]
Opposes shutdowns and supports bipartisan governance [S1][S2]

spending.basis: He supports restoring federal-agency capacity rather than reducing it.

experience.basis: He offers military and journalism experience but no elected record.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Kevin Fagan
candidates.summary: Fagan is a filed Democratic candidate whose reviewed dossier documents only general affordability and representation priorities.
highlights: Official statement emphasizes affordability and representation [S1]

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Michael Baumgartner
candidates.summary: Baumgartner is the freshman incumbent and a former state senator and county treasurer emphasizing dams, agriculture, water, constituent service, and cross-party work.
highlights: Has served in Congress, the state Senate, and as county treasurer [S1][S2]
Supports Snake River dams, agriculture, water resources, and congressional integrity [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He is the incumbent with substantial prior state and county elected service.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Kyle Usrey
candidates.summary: Usrey is a lawyer and former university president advocating single-payer major-medical coverage, limits on ICE misconduct, AI regulation, and institutional term limits.
highlights: Supports single-payer major-medical reform [S1][S2]
Supports civil liability for unlawful ICE conduct, AI regulation, and Supreme Court term limits [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He has extensive professional and institutional leadership but no documented elected record.

reform.basis: He supports executive limits and Supreme Court term limits.

immigration.basis: He supports civil liability for unlawful ICE conduct.

healthcare.basis: He explicitly supports single-payer major-medical reform.

tech.basis: He explicitly supports AI regulation.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Andrew Bartleson
candidates.summary: Bartleson is a first-time candidate advocating universal health and dental coverage, antitrust action, political disclosure, and AI controls.
highlights: Supports universal health and dental coverage [S1][S2]
Supports stock-trading disclosure, antitrust action, and AI privacy and price-fixing controls [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate without an elected record.

reform.basis: He supports stock-trading disclosure as a political-accountability reform.

tech.basis: He supports AI privacy and price-fixing controls.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Ann Marie Danimus
candidates.summary: Danimus is an independent small-business consultant emphasizing no PAC money, affordability, reproductive rights, balanced immigration policy, and addiction services.
highlights: Rejects PAC money and supports program restructuring [S1][S2]
Supports abortion rights and immigration with border protection [S1][S2]

experience.basis: She offers business and caregiving experience rather than elected office.

reform.basis: She explicitly rejects PAC money and runs independently.

immigration.basis: She combines support for immigration with border protection.

social.basis: She explicitly supports abortion rights.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Richard Freudenberg
candidates.summary: Freudenberg is a retired Air Force lawyer emphasizing health access, rural hospitals, affordability, legal accountability, and opposition to tax cuts concentrated on wealthy people.
highlights: Supports health-care access and rural hospitals [S1][S2]
Opposes tax cuts concentrated on millionaires and billionaires [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: He opposes tax cuts concentrated on millionaires and billionaires.

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate despite extensive military legal experience.

healthcare.basis: He supports access and rural hospitals without proposing single-payer transformation.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: David Womack
candidates.summary: Womack is a retired military medical commander and rural-hospital executive advocating universal coverage, reproductive autonomy, climate and water research, and humane border security.
highlights: Supports universal coverage and sustainable rural hospitals [S1][S2]
Supports reproductive autonomy, climate research, water stewardship, and humane border security [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He has senior military and hospital experience but no elected record.

immigration.basis: His humane-border-security position combines enforcement and protections.

social.basis: He explicitly supports reproductive autonomy.

defense.basis: He supports defense while opposing forever wars.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

contests.office_does: A U.S. representative writes and votes on federal law and spending, conducts oversight, and provides constituent services.
contests.race_blurb: The open-seat field spans experienced officeholders and outsider candidates with differences on taxes, immigration, energy, reform, social policy, technology, and defense.

candidates.name: Jacek "Jack" Kobiesa
candidates.summary: Engineer emphasizing capitalism, liberty, constitutional limits, science, and accountability.
highlights: Engineer emphasizing capitalism, liberty, constitutional limits, science, and accountability. [S1]

experience.basis: First-time outsider.

candidates.name: Amanda McKinney
candidates.summary: County commissioner emphasizing agriculture, affordable conventional energy, enforcement, gun rights, and fiscal responsibility.
highlights: County commissioner emphasizing agriculture, affordable conventional energy, enforcement, gun rights, and fiscal responsibility. [S1]

spending.basis: Fiscal-responsibility and fraud-prevention emphasis.

safety.basis: Explicit law-enforcement priority.

climate.basis: Dams, nuclear, fusion, LNG and affordability-first posture.

immigration.basis: Explicit border enforcement.

experience.basis: Six years as county commissioner and national county leadership.

candidates.name: John Duresky
candidates.summary: Veteran and federal technology manager supporting labor, infrastructure, current health programs moving universal, ethics reform, and public lands.
highlights: Veteran and federal technology manager supporting labor, infrastructure, current health programs moving universal, ethics reform, and public lands. [S1]

spending.basis: Infrastructure, broadband, rural hospitals and subsidies.

reform.basis: Stock ban, Citizens United reversal, ethics rules.

healthcare.basis: Current-system protections with eventual universal coverage.

experience.basis: Substantial federal management but no elected record.

candidates.name: John C. Hughs
candidates.summary: Farmer, lawyer, engineer, and small-business candidate emphasizing debt, tax breaks, stewardship, family, church, and property rights.
highlights: Farmer, lawyer, engineer, and small-business candidate emphasizing debt, tax breaks, stewardship, family, church, and property rights. [S1]

taxes.basis: Small-business tax breaks.

spending.basis: National-debt reduction.

experience.basis: No elected record.

candidates.name: Favian Valencia
candidates.summary: Civil-rights attorney emphasizing constitutional protections and independent, cross-partisan governance.
highlights: Civil-rights attorney emphasizing constitutional protections and independent, cross-partisan governance. [S1]

experience.basis: First-time candidate.

candidates.name: Jerrod Sessler
candidates.summary: Repeat America First candidate opposing tax-and-spend policy and emphasizing faith, family, constitutional government, and anti-establishment politics.
highlights: Repeat America First candidate opposing tax-and-spend policy and emphasizing faith, family, constitutional government, and anti-establishment politics. [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicit opposition to taxes and property taxes.

spending.basis: Explicit anti-spending platform.

social.basis: Faith-and-family traditional-values platform.

experience.basis: Repeat candidate and primary winner but no officeholding record.

candidates.name: Devin Poore
candidates.summary: Software engineer supporting structural reform, public investment, clean energy, dignified immigration, and reduced foreign wars.
highlights: Software engineer supporting structural reform, public investment, clean energy, dignified immigration, and reduced foreign wars. [S1]

spending.basis: Farm stabilization and workforce programs.

climate.basis: Decentralized clean energy and wildfire management.

reform.basis: Term limits, stock ban, finance reform, lobbying transparency.

immigration.basis: Dignified immigration policy.

defense.basis: Opposes costly wars and Gaza policy.

experience.basis: No elected record.

candidates.name: Ken Vaz
candidates.summary: Technology entrepreneur emphasizing budgets, public safety, affordable energy, jobs, and innovation with worker protections.
highlights: Technology entrepreneur emphasizing budgets, public safety, affordable energy, jobs, and innovation with worker protections. [S1]

spending.basis: Responsible-budgeting emphasis.

safety.basis: Public-safety priority without detailed mechanism.

climate.basis: Energy affordability emphasis.

tech.basis: Innovation-friendly AI posture with worker protections.

experience.basis: First-time candidate.

candidates.name: Zac Rossi
candidates.summary: Nuclear operator proposing spending caps, subsidy reductions, later eligibility, Medicare competition, and targeted tax increases.
highlights: Nuclear operator proposing spending caps, subsidy reductions, later eligibility, Medicare competition, and targeted tax increases. [S1]

taxes.basis: Combines top-bracket and payroll increases with other restraint.

spending.basis: Specific spending caps, subsidy reductions, and later eligibility.

healthcare.basis: More private Medicare competition within current system.

experience.basis: No elected record.

candidates.name: Elpidia Saavedra
candidates.summary: Mayor combining infrastructure, safety, health, housing, clean energy, immigration reform, parental rights, lower taxes, and donor independence.
highlights: Mayor combining infrastructure, safety, health, housing, clean energy, immigration reform, parental rights, lower taxes, and donor independence. [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicit lower-tax position.

spending.basis: Infrastructure and public-safety funding.

climate.basis: Renewable-energy and clean-water support.

reform.basis: Independence from large donors.

social.basis: Parental-rights and faith-family platform.

experience.basis: Mayor and council record.

candidates.name: Matt Boehnke
candidates.summary: State senator and veteran emphasizing lower taxes, less regulation, conventional energy, border enforcement, fentanyl interdiction, faith, and family.
highlights: State senator and veteran emphasizing lower taxes, less regulation, conventional energy, border enforcement, fentanyl interdiction, faith, and family. [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicit lower taxes.

safety.basis: Fentanyl interdiction and enforcement.

climate.basis: Hydropower, nuclear, dams and affordability.

immigration.basis: Border enforcement.

experience.basis: Eight years legislative and prior council record.

contests.office_does: A U.S. representative writes and votes on federal law and spending, conducts oversight, and provides constituent services.
contests.race_blurb: First-term incumbent Emily Randall faces an anti-party independent, a fiscal and public-safety Republican, a social-investment and demilitarization candidate, and an America First challenger.

candidates.name: Emily Randall
candidates.summary: The first-term incumbent emphasizes Medicaid protection, cost reduction, oversight, and bipartisan investment in district infrastructure and services.
highlights: Prioritizes reducing costs and protecting Medicaid [S1][S2]
Reports securing nearly $18 million for district projects [S1]
Emphasizes bipartisan housing, ferry, infrastructure, tribal, and military-family projects [S1]

spending.basis: Her record and platform emphasize federal investment in district infrastructure, housing, emergency management, and services.

experience.basis: She has a current congressional voting and oversight record, though she is a first-term incumbent.

reform.basis: She emphasizes institutional coalition-building, bipartisan caucus work, and established organizational support.

immigration.basis: She opposes Trump administration immigration actions, without advocating the structural abolition proposed by Jones.

healthcare.basis: She prioritizes protecting Medicaid and reducing costs within the existing system.

sources.ref: Kitsap County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 12

candidates.name: Brian P. O'Gorman
candidates.summary: O'Gorman centers an anti-two-party, anti-insider critique and calls for reforms involving civil forfeiture, qualified immunity, and gerrymandering.
highlights: Served 16 years as an Army and National Guard field medic [S1]
Says party and insider arrangements distort representation [S1][S2]
Criticizes civil forfeiture, qualified immunity, and gerrymandering [S1]

experience.basis: He offers military and volunteer experience but no elected record.

reform.basis: His campaign centrally attacks two-party, insider, gerrymandering, and qualified-immunity structures.

sources.ref: Kitsap County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 13

candidates.name: Teresa Fox
candidates.summary: Fox presents a conventional Republican platform focused on lower taxes, balanced budgets, law enforcement, anti-trafficking efforts, and maritime and naval employment.
highlights: Supports lower taxes and balanced budgets [S1][S2]
Supports law enforcement, first responders, and stronger action against drugs and trafficking [S1][S2]
Reports 30 years in compliance, safety, risk management, and project budgets [S1]

taxes.basis: She explicitly supports lower taxes.

spending.basis: She explicitly prioritizes balanced budgets.

safety.basis: She emphasizes law enforcement, first responders, and enforcement against drugs and trafficking.

experience.basis: She has extensive professional and party-service experience but no elected record.

defense.basis: Explicitly prioritizes building naval dominance and supporting the district's maritime and naval industries, aligning with maintained defense commitments and defense-industry partnership.

sources.ref: Kitsap County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 13

candidates.name: Macy Jones
candidates.summary: Jones advances the field's broadest social-investment platform and its clearest positions for abolishing ICE, reducing overseas-war spending, and regulating AI.
highlights: Supports universal health care, labor rights, schools, infrastructure, elder care, and food security [S1][S2]
Supports abolishing ICE and detention centers and limiting surveillance [S1]
Opposes overseas-war spending and rejects AIPAC funding [S1]

spending.basis: She proposes broad public investment across health care, labor, schools, infrastructure, elder care, and food security.

climate.basis: She supports environmental protection and tribal partnerships to protect forests and rivers.

experience.basis: She brings health-care, elder-care, and community experience but no elected record.

immigration.basis: She explicitly supports abolishing ICE and detention centers.

healthcare.basis: She explicitly supports universal health care.

tech.basis: She favors surveillance limits and responsible AI regulation.

defense.basis: She opposes overseas-war spending and rejects AIPAC funding.

reform.basis: Rejects AIPAC and foreign money and says she would represent constituents rather than corporate donors or political insiders, directly engaging institutional campaign money.

social.basis: Explicitly supports gender equality, marriage equality, voting rights, privacy, and related progressive rights protections.

sources.ref: Kitsap County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 14

candidates.name: Leon Lawson
candidates.summary: Lawson offers an America First populist platform with juvenile workforce rehabilitation, tribal empowerment, and targeted health-care and technology proposals.
highlights: Supports secure elections, tribal empowerment, and juvenile rehabilitation tied to workforce development [S1][S2]
Proposes changes aimed at pharmaceutical intermediaries [S1]
Previously ran for governor and U.S. Senate but has not held elected office [S1]

safety.basis: His documented public-safety proposal emphasizes juvenile rehabilitation through workforce development.

experience.basis: He is a repeat outsider candidate without an elected record.

reform.basis: His America First candidacy is explicitly populist and anti-establishment.

sources.ref: Kitsap County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 14

contests.office_does: A U.S. representative writes and votes on federal law and spending, conducts executive oversight, and provides constituent services.
contests.race_blurb: Long-serving incumbent Rick Larsen faces Edwin Feller's smaller-government campaign and two progressive challengers, Tomas Scheel and Devin Hermanson, who advocate more structural changes.

candidates.name: Edwin H. Feller
candidates.summary: Feller campaigns on smaller government, lower taxes, balanced budgets, border enforcement, law enforcement, and traditional Title IX protections.
highlights: Attributes affordability problems to taxes and government growth [S1]
Supports border security, law enforcement, election security, and balanced budgets [S1][S2]
Offers extensive private-sector work but no elected record [S1]

taxes.basis: He explicitly identifies high taxes and government growth as causes of affordability problems.

spending.basis: He emphasizes smaller government and balanced budgets.

safety.basis: He supports law enforcement as a central public-safety approach.

experience.basis: He offers private-sector and overseas facilities experience but no elected record.

immigration.basis: He explicitly prioritizes border security and enforcement.

social.basis: His Title IX position is framed around traditional sex-based protections for women.

sources.ref: Snohomish County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 16

candidates.name: Tomas Scheel
candidates.summary: Scheel combines universal health care and clean-energy investment with progressive social policy, replacing ICE, and restrictions on institutional campaign money.
highlights: Supports universal health care, clean energy, and major housing investment [S1][S2]
Calls for dismantling ICE and replacing it with a rights-grounded system [S1]
Rejects corporate PAC and foreign-lobby money [S1]

spending.basis: He supports major federal investment in health care, housing, and clean energy.

climate.basis: He explicitly supports a clean-energy transition.

experience.basis: He is an outsider candidate whose experience is military, technical, business, and volunteer service rather than elected office.

reform.basis: He rejects corporate PAC and foreign-lobby money and calls for higher official-accountability standards.

immigration.basis: He calls for dismantling ICE and replacing it with a rights-grounded immigration system.

healthcare.basis: He explicitly supports universal health care.

social.basis: He supports reproductive freedom and access to gender-affirming care.

sources.ref: Snohomish County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 17

candidates.name: Devin Hermanson
candidates.summary: Hermanson runs to the incumbent's left on wealth taxation, universal health care, climate investment, institutional reform, and U.S. aid to Israel.
highlights: Supports taxing billionaires, Medicare for All, and the Green New Deal [S1][S2]
Rejects corporate money and supports Supreme Court reform [S1][S2]
Calls for ending U.S. aid to Israel [S1]

taxes.basis: He explicitly supports taxing billionaires to fund a broader public agenda.

spending.basis: The Green New Deal and Medicare for All place expanded public investment at the center of his platform.

climate.basis: He explicitly supports the Green New Deal.

experience.basis: He is a challenger without elected office who frames his campaign around institutional rebuilding.

reform.basis: He rejects corporate money and calls for structural Supreme Court reform.

healthcare.basis: He explicitly supports Medicare for All.

defense.basis: He calls for ending U.S. aid to Israel.

sources.ref: Snohomish County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 18

candidates.name: Rick Larsen
candidates.summary: The long-serving incumbent emphasizes incremental expansion of health-care access, housing and infrastructure investment, fentanyl enforcement, and legal constraints on ICE.
highlights: Supports expanding health-care access and lowering costs [S1][S2]
Supports affordable-housing supply, infrastructure, jobs, and childcare [S1]
Supports fentanyl enforcement while requiring ICE agents to follow ordinary law-enforcement rules [S1]

spending.basis: He supports federal investment in housing, infrastructure, jobs, and childcare.

safety.basis: He combines law-enforcement action against fentanyl with constraints on immigration agents.

experience.basis: He is a long-serving incumbent with an extensive congressional record.

immigration.basis: He opposes executive actions targeting immigrant communities and supports law-enforcement rules for ICE.

healthcare.basis: He proposes expanding access and lowering costs within the current health-care system.

sources.ref: Snohomish County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 19

contests.office_does: A U.S. representative writes and votes on federal law and spending, conducts oversight, and provides constituent services.
contests.race_blurb: First-term incumbent Emily Randall faces an anti-party independent, a fiscal and public-safety Republican, a social-investment and demilitarization candidate, and an America First challenger.

candidates.name: Emily Randall
candidates.summary: The first-term incumbent emphasizes Medicaid protection, cost reduction, oversight, and bipartisan investment in district infrastructure and services.
highlights: Prioritizes reducing costs and protecting Medicaid [S1][S2]
Reports securing nearly $18 million for district projects [S1]
Emphasizes bipartisan housing, ferry, infrastructure, tribal, and military-family projects [S1]

spending.basis: Her record and platform emphasize federal investment in district infrastructure, housing, emergency management, and services.

experience.basis: She has a current congressional voting and oversight record, though she is a first-term incumbent.

reform.basis: She emphasizes institutional coalition-building, bipartisan caucus work, and established organizational support.

immigration.basis: She opposes Trump administration immigration actions, without advocating the structural abolition proposed by Jones.

healthcare.basis: She prioritizes protecting Medicaid and reducing costs within the existing system.

sources.ref: Kitsap County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 12

candidates.name: Brian P. O'Gorman
candidates.summary: O'Gorman centers an anti-two-party, anti-insider critique and calls for reforms involving civil forfeiture, qualified immunity, and gerrymandering.
highlights: Served 16 years as an Army and National Guard field medic [S1]
Says party and insider arrangements distort representation [S1][S2]
Criticizes civil forfeiture, qualified immunity, and gerrymandering [S1]

experience.basis: He offers military and volunteer experience but no elected record.

reform.basis: His campaign centrally attacks two-party, insider, gerrymandering, and qualified-immunity structures.

sources.ref: Kitsap County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 13

candidates.name: Teresa Fox
candidates.summary: Fox presents a conventional Republican platform focused on lower taxes, balanced budgets, law enforcement, anti-trafficking efforts, and maritime and naval employment.
highlights: Supports lower taxes and balanced budgets [S1][S2]
Supports law enforcement, first responders, and stronger action against drugs and trafficking [S1][S2]
Reports 30 years in compliance, safety, risk management, and project budgets [S1]

taxes.basis: She explicitly supports lower taxes.

spending.basis: She explicitly prioritizes balanced budgets.

safety.basis: She emphasizes law enforcement, first responders, and enforcement against drugs and trafficking.

experience.basis: She has extensive professional and party-service experience but no elected record.

defense.basis: Explicitly prioritizes building naval dominance and supporting the district's maritime and naval industries, aligning with maintained defense commitments and defense-industry partnership.

sources.ref: Kitsap County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 13

candidates.name: Macy Jones
candidates.summary: Jones advances the field's broadest social-investment platform and its clearest positions for abolishing ICE, reducing overseas-war spending, and regulating AI.
highlights: Supports universal health care, labor rights, schools, infrastructure, elder care, and food security [S1][S2]
Supports abolishing ICE and detention centers and limiting surveillance [S1]
Opposes overseas-war spending and rejects AIPAC funding [S1]

spending.basis: She proposes broad public investment across health care, labor, schools, infrastructure, elder care, and food security.

climate.basis: She supports environmental protection and tribal partnerships to protect forests and rivers.

experience.basis: She brings health-care, elder-care, and community experience but no elected record.

immigration.basis: She explicitly supports abolishing ICE and detention centers.

healthcare.basis: She explicitly supports universal health care.

tech.basis: She favors surveillance limits and responsible AI regulation.

defense.basis: She opposes overseas-war spending and rejects AIPAC funding.

reform.basis: Rejects AIPAC and foreign money and says she would represent constituents rather than corporate donors or political insiders, directly engaging institutional campaign money.

social.basis: Explicitly supports gender equality, marriage equality, voting rights, privacy, and related progressive rights protections.

sources.ref: Kitsap County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 14

candidates.name: Leon Lawson
candidates.summary: Lawson offers an America First populist platform with juvenile workforce rehabilitation, tribal empowerment, and targeted health-care and technology proposals.
highlights: Supports secure elections, tribal empowerment, and juvenile rehabilitation tied to workforce development [S1][S2]
Proposes changes aimed at pharmaceutical intermediaries [S1]
Previously ran for governor and U.S. Senate but has not held elected office [S1]

safety.basis: His documented public-safety proposal emphasizes juvenile rehabilitation through workforce development.

experience.basis: He is a repeat outsider candidate without an elected record.

reform.basis: His America First candidacy is explicitly populist and anti-establishment.

sources.ref: Kitsap County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 14

contests.office_does: The assessor values taxable property and administers appeals and exemptions without setting tax rates.
contests.race_blurb: Incumbent Phil Cook emphasizes administrative continuity and workflow efficiency; Michael Simonds emphasizes data modernization, valuation equity, and accessible tools.

candidates.name: Phil Cook
candidates.summary: The incumbent assessor, emphasizing valuation accuracy, technology, fiscal responsibility, and a recognized exemption workflow.
highlights: Has served as assessor since 2015 after a long local real-estate career [S1]
Says the exemption workflow became a state-recommended practice [S1]

spending.basis: Explicitly emphasizes fiscal responsibility and efficient technology adoption.

experience.basis: Incumbent since 2015 with a documented administrative and exemption-program record.

sources.ref: Kitsap County 2026 primary local voters' pamphlet candidate statement

candidates.name: Michael Simonds
candidates.summary: A Navy veteran and real-estate professional proposing data-driven valuation review and accessible appeal and exemption tools.
highlights: Proposes analytics to correct neighborhood-level valuation imbalances [S1]
Supports plain-language valuation and appeal tools plus exemption outreach [S1]

experience.basis: Offers relevant real-estate analysis but no assessor-office administration or elected record.

sources.ref: Kitsap County 2026 primary local voters' pamphlet candidate statement

contests.office_does: The county commission adopts budgets and policy and oversees services, land use, infrastructure, and public health.
contests.race_blurb: Incumbent Katie Walters emphasizes transit, health care, infrastructure, and collaboration; Kevin Tisdel emphasizes process discipline, affordability, rural character, and controlled growth.

candidates.name: Kevin Tisdel
candidates.summary: A business owner and former civil-service manager emphasizing outcome-focused processes, affordability, rural character, and paced growth.
highlights: Calls for outcome-focused county processes and affordability [S1]
Supports protecting rural and recreational character and controlling growth pace [S1]

spending.basis: Emphasizes outcome discipline and affordability rather than expansion.

experience.basis: Has utility-management and business experience but no documented elected county record.

sources.ref: Kitsap County 2026 primary local voters' pamphlet candidate statement

candidates.name: Katie Walters
candidates.summary: The incumbent commissioner, citing work on health-care training, transit, permitting, broadband, infrastructure, and workforce development.
highlights: Supports expanded transit and ferry service, health-care access, broadband, and infrastructure [S1]
Supports environmental protection and rural character [S1]

spending.basis: Uses public and regional investment in transit, health care, broadband, and infrastructure as key tools.

climate.basis: Explicitly supports environmental protection alongside infrastructure and rural character.

experience.basis: Incumbent with documented county and regional-board work across several policy areas.

sources.ref: Kitsap County 2026 primary local voters' pamphlet candidate statement

contests.office_does: The clerk maintains Superior Court records and provides court-user, filing, and passport services.
contests.race_blurb: Incumbent David Lewis offers 41 years in the office and an e-filing record; deputy clerk Brien Kennedy proposes audits, guides, and public office hours.

candidates.name: David T Lewis III
candidates.summary: The incumbent clerk, emphasizing continuity, e-filing, customer service, fairness, and access to justice.
highlights: Reports 41 years in the clerk's office [S1]
Implemented remote electronic filing [S1]

experience.basis: Incumbent clerk with four decades of direct office experience and an implemented e-filing record.

sources.ref: Kitsap County 2026 primary local voters' pamphlet candidate statement

candidates.name: Brien Kennedy
candidates.summary: A deputy clerk and former private-sector manager running on audits, public visibility, and plain-language service.
highlights: Proposes a public operational audit and plain-language guides [S1]
Would hold monthly public office hours [S1]

spending.basis: Makes a public operational audit and accountability the central administrative proposal.

experience.basis: Combines current deputy-clerk experience with an outsider management and renewal campaign.

sources.ref: Kitsap County 2026 primary local voters' pamphlet candidate statement

contests.office_does: The prosecutor brings criminal cases, represents and advises the county, and administers the prosecutor's office.
contests.race_blurb: Incumbent Chad Enright emphasizes diversion, treatment courts, data, and administration; Joe Lombardi combines violent-crime prosecution with limited diversion and mental-health services.

candidates.name: Joe Lombardi
candidates.summary: A former prosecutor, assistant attorney general, and public defender proposing direct violent-crime prosecution plus diversion for lower-level cases.
highlights: Would personally prosecute serious violent offenses [S1]
Supports diversion for low-level and first-time offenses and mental-health services [S1]
Would keep ICE outside county cases [S1]

safety.basis: Genuinely combines enforcement for serious violence with diversion and mental-health services for lower-level cases.

experience.basis: Has substantial prosecution, public-defense, and public-law experience but no incumbent management record.

sources.ref: Kitsap County 2026 primary local voters' pamphlet candidate statement

candidates.name: Chad M. Enright
candidates.summary: The incumbent prosecutor, supporting diversion, recovery resources, specialty courts, data transparency, and race-blind charging.
highlights: Supports pre-arrest diversion, treatment and specialty courts, and recovery resources [S1]
Reports paperless operations, full staffing, no backlog, and expanded specialty courts [S1]

safety.basis: Repeatedly centers diversion, treatment courts, recovery resources, and race-blind charging.

experience.basis: County prosecutor since 2019 after service as chief deputy and office attorney.

sources.ref: Kitsap County 2026 primary local voters' pamphlet candidate statement

contests.office_does: The sheriff leads county patrol, investigations, emergency response, law-enforcement partnerships, and agency administration.
contests.race_blurb: Supervisor Brandon Myers emphasizes staffing, professionalism, and partnerships; deputy Rick Kuss emphasizes fentanyl enforcement, visible policing, rights, and accountability.

candidates.name: Rick Kuss
candidates.summary: A deputy and military veteran emphasizing proactive fentanyl enforcement, trafficking operations, victim support, visible policing, and accountability.
highlights: Prioritizes proactive fentanyl enforcement and regional anti-trafficking operations [S1]
Supports visible evidence-based policing, victim support, constitutional rights, and accountability [S1]

safety.basis: Platform centrally emphasizes proactive enforcement, visible policing, trafficking operations, and child protection.

experience.basis: Has deputy, military-leadership, and co-responder experience but no sheriff-office executive record.

sources.ref: Kitsap County 2026 primary local voters' pamphlet candidate statement

candidates.name: Brandon L. Myers
candidates.summary: A sheriff's-office supervisor emphasizing staffing, retention, equipment, partnerships, transparency, and professionalism.
highlights: Has more than 20 years across dispatch, patrol, investigations, crisis intervention, and training [S1]
Prioritizes staffing, equipment, partnerships, transparency, and accountability [S1]

safety.basis: Combines core law-enforcement staffing and investigations with crisis-intervention and partnership experience.

experience.basis: Long agency tenure and current major-crime supervisory responsibility support a proven-record lean.

sources.ref: Kitsap County 2026 primary local voters' pamphlet candidate statement

contests.office_does: A U.S. representative writes and votes on federal law and spending, conducts oversight, and provides constituent services.
contests.race_blurb: The open-seat field spans experienced officeholders and outsider candidates with differences on taxes, immigration, energy, reform, social policy, technology, and defense.

candidates.name: Jacek "Jack" Kobiesa
candidates.summary: Engineer emphasizing capitalism, liberty, constitutional limits, science, and accountability.
highlights: Engineer emphasizing capitalism, liberty, constitutional limits, science, and accountability. [S1]

experience.basis: First-time outsider.

candidates.name: Amanda McKinney
candidates.summary: County commissioner emphasizing agriculture, affordable conventional energy, enforcement, gun rights, and fiscal responsibility.
highlights: County commissioner emphasizing agriculture, affordable conventional energy, enforcement, gun rights, and fiscal responsibility. [S1]

spending.basis: Fiscal-responsibility and fraud-prevention emphasis.

safety.basis: Explicit law-enforcement priority.

climate.basis: Dams, nuclear, fusion, LNG and affordability-first posture.

immigration.basis: Explicit border enforcement.

experience.basis: Six years as county commissioner and national county leadership.

candidates.name: John Duresky
candidates.summary: Veteran and federal technology manager supporting labor, infrastructure, current health programs moving universal, ethics reform, and public lands.
highlights: Veteran and federal technology manager supporting labor, infrastructure, current health programs moving universal, ethics reform, and public lands. [S1]

spending.basis: Infrastructure, broadband, rural hospitals and subsidies.

reform.basis: Stock ban, Citizens United reversal, ethics rules.

healthcare.basis: Current-system protections with eventual universal coverage.

experience.basis: Substantial federal management but no elected record.

candidates.name: John C. Hughs
candidates.summary: Farmer, lawyer, engineer, and small-business candidate emphasizing debt, tax breaks, stewardship, family, church, and property rights.
highlights: Farmer, lawyer, engineer, and small-business candidate emphasizing debt, tax breaks, stewardship, family, church, and property rights. [S1]

taxes.basis: Small-business tax breaks.

spending.basis: National-debt reduction.

experience.basis: No elected record.

candidates.name: Favian Valencia
candidates.summary: Civil-rights attorney emphasizing constitutional protections and independent, cross-partisan governance.
highlights: Civil-rights attorney emphasizing constitutional protections and independent, cross-partisan governance. [S1]

experience.basis: First-time candidate.

candidates.name: Jerrod Sessler
candidates.summary: Repeat America First candidate opposing tax-and-spend policy and emphasizing faith, family, constitutional government, and anti-establishment politics.
highlights: Repeat America First candidate opposing tax-and-spend policy and emphasizing faith, family, constitutional government, and anti-establishment politics. [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicit opposition to taxes and property taxes.

spending.basis: Explicit anti-spending platform.

social.basis: Faith-and-family traditional-values platform.

experience.basis: Repeat candidate and primary winner but no officeholding record.

candidates.name: Devin Poore
candidates.summary: Software engineer supporting structural reform, public investment, clean energy, dignified immigration, and reduced foreign wars.
highlights: Software engineer supporting structural reform, public investment, clean energy, dignified immigration, and reduced foreign wars. [S1]

spending.basis: Farm stabilization and workforce programs.

climate.basis: Decentralized clean energy and wildfire management.

reform.basis: Term limits, stock ban, finance reform, lobbying transparency.

immigration.basis: Dignified immigration policy.

defense.basis: Opposes costly wars and Gaza policy.

experience.basis: No elected record.

candidates.name: Ken Vaz
candidates.summary: Technology entrepreneur emphasizing budgets, public safety, affordable energy, jobs, and innovation with worker protections.
highlights: Technology entrepreneur emphasizing budgets, public safety, affordable energy, jobs, and innovation with worker protections. [S1]

spending.basis: Responsible-budgeting emphasis.

safety.basis: Public-safety priority without detailed mechanism.

climate.basis: Energy affordability emphasis.

tech.basis: Innovation-friendly AI posture with worker protections.

experience.basis: First-time candidate.

candidates.name: Zac Rossi
candidates.summary: Nuclear operator proposing spending caps, subsidy reductions, later eligibility, Medicare competition, and targeted tax increases.
highlights: Nuclear operator proposing spending caps, subsidy reductions, later eligibility, Medicare competition, and targeted tax increases. [S1]

taxes.basis: Combines top-bracket and payroll increases with other restraint.

spending.basis: Specific spending caps, subsidy reductions, and later eligibility.

healthcare.basis: More private Medicare competition within current system.

experience.basis: No elected record.

candidates.name: Elpidia Saavedra
candidates.summary: Mayor combining infrastructure, safety, health, housing, clean energy, immigration reform, parental rights, lower taxes, and donor independence.
highlights: Mayor combining infrastructure, safety, health, housing, clean energy, immigration reform, parental rights, lower taxes, and donor independence. [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicit lower-tax position.

spending.basis: Infrastructure and public-safety funding.

climate.basis: Renewable-energy and clean-water support.

reform.basis: Independence from large donors.

social.basis: Parental-rights and faith-family platform.

experience.basis: Mayor and council record.

candidates.name: Matt Boehnke
candidates.summary: State senator and veteran emphasizing lower taxes, less regulation, conventional energy, border enforcement, fentanyl interdiction, faith, and family.
highlights: State senator and veteran emphasizing lower taxes, less regulation, conventional energy, border enforcement, fentanyl interdiction, faith, and family. [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicit lower taxes.

safety.basis: Fentanyl interdiction and enforcement.

climate.basis: Hydropower, nuclear, dams and affordability.

immigration.basis: Border enforcement.

experience.basis: Eight years legislative and prior council record.
