contests.office_does: A U.S. representative writes and votes on federal law and spending, conducts executive oversight, and provides constituent services.
contests.race_blurb: Long-serving incumbent Rick Larsen faces Edwin Feller's smaller-government campaign and two progressive challengers, Tomas Scheel and Devin Hermanson, who advocate more structural changes.

candidates.name: Edwin H. Feller
candidates.summary: Feller campaigns on smaller government, lower taxes, balanced budgets, border enforcement, law enforcement, and traditional Title IX protections.
highlights: Attributes affordability problems to taxes and government growth [S1]
Supports border security, law enforcement, election security, and balanced budgets [S1][S2]
Offers extensive private-sector work but no elected record [S1]

taxes.basis: He explicitly identifies high taxes and government growth as causes of affordability problems.

spending.basis: He emphasizes smaller government and balanced budgets.

safety.basis: He supports law enforcement as a central public-safety approach.

experience.basis: He offers private-sector and overseas facilities experience but no elected record.

immigration.basis: He explicitly prioritizes border security and enforcement.

social.basis: His Title IX position is framed around traditional sex-based protections for women.

sources.ref: Snohomish County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 16

candidates.name: Tomas Scheel
candidates.summary: Scheel combines universal health care and clean-energy investment with progressive social policy, replacing ICE, and restrictions on institutional campaign money.
highlights: Supports universal health care, clean energy, and major housing investment [S1][S2]
Calls for dismantling ICE and replacing it with a rights-grounded system [S1]
Rejects corporate PAC and foreign-lobby money [S1]

spending.basis: He supports major federal investment in health care, housing, and clean energy.

climate.basis: He explicitly supports a clean-energy transition.

experience.basis: He is an outsider candidate whose experience is military, technical, business, and volunteer service rather than elected office.

reform.basis: He rejects corporate PAC and foreign-lobby money and calls for higher official-accountability standards.

immigration.basis: He calls for dismantling ICE and replacing it with a rights-grounded immigration system.

healthcare.basis: He explicitly supports universal health care.

social.basis: He supports reproductive freedom and access to gender-affirming care.

sources.ref: Snohomish County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 17

candidates.name: Devin Hermanson
candidates.summary: Hermanson runs to the incumbent's left on wealth taxation, universal health care, climate investment, institutional reform, and U.S. aid to Israel.
highlights: Supports taxing billionaires, Medicare for All, and the Green New Deal [S1][S2]
Rejects corporate money and supports Supreme Court reform [S1][S2]
Calls for ending U.S. aid to Israel [S1]

taxes.basis: He explicitly supports taxing billionaires to fund a broader public agenda.

spending.basis: The Green New Deal and Medicare for All place expanded public investment at the center of his platform.

climate.basis: He explicitly supports the Green New Deal.

experience.basis: He is a challenger without elected office who frames his campaign around institutional rebuilding.

reform.basis: He rejects corporate money and calls for structural Supreme Court reform.

healthcare.basis: He explicitly supports Medicare for All.

defense.basis: He calls for ending U.S. aid to Israel.

sources.ref: Snohomish County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 18

candidates.name: Rick Larsen
candidates.summary: The long-serving incumbent emphasizes incremental expansion of health-care access, housing and infrastructure investment, fentanyl enforcement, and legal constraints on ICE.
highlights: Supports expanding health-care access and lowering costs [S1][S2]
Supports affordable-housing supply, infrastructure, jobs, and childcare [S1]
Supports fentanyl enforcement while requiring ICE agents to follow ordinary law-enforcement rules [S1]

spending.basis: He supports federal investment in housing, infrastructure, jobs, and childcare.

safety.basis: He combines law-enforcement action against fentanyl with constraints on immigration agents.

experience.basis: He is a long-serving incumbent with an extensive congressional record.

immigration.basis: He opposes executive actions targeting immigrant communities and supports law-enforcement rules for ICE.

healthcare.basis: He proposes expanding access and lowering costs within the current health-care system.

sources.ref: Snohomish County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 19

contests.office_does: A U.S. representative writes and votes on federal law and spending, conducts executive oversight, and provides constituent services.
contests.race_blurb: Incumbent Suzan DelBene faces challengers ranging from progressive structural-change campaigns to independent reform and smaller-government alternatives.

candidates.name: Benjamin Kincaid
candidates.summary: Kincaid is a first-time candidate combining working-class tax relief and domestic production with an incremental approach to health-system change.
highlights: Supports a large working-class income-tax cut and expanded domestic energy and manufacturing [S1][S2]
Supports pilot-tested incrementalism before national single-payer implementation [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: He explicitly supports a large working-class income-tax cut.

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate without an institutional record.

healthcare.basis: He favors pilots and incremental steps before a national single-payer rollout.

sources.ref: Washington 2026 primary candidate statement archived in county voters' pamphlets

candidates.name: Suzan DelBene
candidates.summary: DelBene is the long-serving incumbent, emphasizing targeted tax credits, housing-supply incentives, current-system health coverage, privacy, and district investment.
highlights: Supports targeted tax credits and affordable-housing supply incentives [S1][S2]
Supports protecting the ACA and Medicaid and strengthening data privacy [S1][S2]
Offers a long congressional and technology-sector record [S1][S2]

spending.basis: She supports federal district investment while using targeted incentives and credits.

experience.basis: She is the long-serving incumbent with an established federal record.

healthcare.basis: She supports protecting and improving ACA and Medicaid coverage within the current system.

tech.basis: She supports stronger federal data-privacy protections.

sources.ref: Washington 2026 primary candidate statement archived in county voters' pamphlets

candidates.name: Bryce Nickel
candidates.summary: Nickel proposes structural public intervention on health care, wealth inequality, rents, and AI-driven economic displacement.
highlights: Supports Medicare for All and wealth-focused taxation [S1][S2]
Supports strong rent intervention and public control of AI and automation [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: He explicitly supports wealth-focused taxation.

spending.basis: His platform centers public provision and intervention in major economic systems.

experience.basis: He is a challenger without a documented institutional record.

healthcare.basis: He explicitly supports Medicare for All.

tech.basis: He supports public control of AI and automation.

sources.ref: Washington 2026 primary candidate statement archived in county voters' pamphlets

candidates.name: James Etzkorn
candidates.summary: Etzkorn combines local elected experience with an independent campaign focused on term limits, dark-money restrictions, and official accountability.
highlights: Has served on the Monroe School Board [S1][S2]
Supports term limits and bans on dark money and congressional insider trading [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He combines elected local service with outsider status in federal politics.

reform.basis: His platform centers term limits, dark-money restrictions, and an insider-trading ban.

sources.ref: Washington 2026 primary candidate statement archived in county voters' pamphlets

candidates.name: Hunter Gordon
candidates.summary: Gordon campaigns on a broad progressive program spanning wealth taxes, universal health care, climate investment, technology regulation, and ending aid to Israel.
highlights: Supports Medicare for All, wealth taxes, and rent stabilization [S1][S2]
Supports a Green New Deal and tighter AI and data-center regulation [S1][S2]
Calls for ending U.S. aid to Israel [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: He explicitly supports wealth taxes.

spending.basis: Medicare for All and a Green New Deal place expanded public investment at the center of his platform.

climate.basis: He explicitly supports a Green New Deal.

experience.basis: He is a challenger without a documented elected record.

reform.basis: His campaign centers opposition to corporate political influence.

healthcare.basis: He explicitly supports Medicare for All.

tech.basis: He supports tighter regulation of AI and data centers.

defense.basis: He calls for ending U.S. aid to Israel.

sources.ref: Washington 2026 primary candidate statement archived in county voters' pamphlets

candidates.name: Mary Silva
candidates.summary: Silva is a small-business owner running on smaller government, enforcement-oriented public safety, domestic production, and traditional values.
highlights: Emphasizes smaller government and domestic production [S1][S2]
Emphasizes public safety, health freedom, and traditional values [S1][S2]

spending.basis: She explicitly emphasizes smaller government.

experience.basis: She offers business and volunteer experience rather than an elected record.

social.basis: Her campaign presents traditional values as a defining alternative.

sources.ref: Washington 2026 primary candidate statement archived in county voters' pamphlets

candidates.name: Catherine Hildebrand
candidates.summary: Hildebrand is a first-time federal candidate advocating universal basic needs and structural limits on lobbying and campaign money.
highlights: Supports universal basic needs including medical care and housing [S1][S2]
Supports term limits and limits on lobbying and campaign money [S1][S2]

spending.basis: She supports public guarantees of basic needs including medical care and housing.

experience.basis: She is a first-time federal candidate from a small-business background.

reform.basis: She supports term limits and restrictions on lobbying and campaign money.

sources.ref: Washington 2026 primary candidate statement archived in county voters' pamphlets

contests.office_does: A U.S. representative serves a two-year term, legislates and votes in the House, conducts oversight, and provides constituent services.
contests.race_blurb: Incumbent Kim Schrier faces five challengers. The dossiers document substantive platforms for Schrier, Keith Arnold, and Bob Hagglund, while evidence for Andres Valleza, Spencer Meline, and Trinh Ha is insufficient for policy scoring.

candidates.name: Kim Schrier
candidates.summary: The incumbent representative, emphasizing health-care costs, abortion access, local police, and federal infrastructure and agricultural-research funding.
highlights: Emphasizes lowering health-care costs [S2]
Supports protecting abortion access and local police [S2]
Highlights federal infrastructure and agricultural-research funding [S2]

spending.basis: Emphasizes securing federal infrastructure and agricultural-research investment.

safety.basis: Explicitly supports local police; no services-first safety plank is documented.

social.basis: Explicitly supports protecting abortion access.

experience.basis: The dossier identifies her as the incumbent who has represented the district since 2019, a maximal proven-tenure profile within this field.

sources.ref: 2026 primary candidate listing

candidates.name: Trinh Ha
candidates.summary: A filed District 8 candidate for whom the reviewed dossier found no verifiable issue positions.
highlights: Identity was matched to the official candidate record and campaign contact [S1][S2]
Policy axes are omitted because the dossier found no verifiable issue position [S1][S2]

sources.ref: 2026 primary candidate listing

candidates.name: Spencer Meline
candidates.summary: A filed District 8 candidate for whom the reviewed dossier found no verifiable issue positions.
highlights: Identity was matched to the official candidate record and campaign contact [S1][S2]
Policy axes are omitted because the dossier found no verifiable issue position [S1][S2]

sources.ref: 2026 primary candidate listing

candidates.name: Keith Arnold
candidates.summary: A challenger with an ideologically mixed platform supporting progressive taxation, universal health care, renewable energy, and political reform.
highlights: Supports ranked-choice voting and campaign reform [S2]
Supports progressive taxation, a living wage, and universal health care [S2]
Supports renewable energy and fair-trade conditions [S2]

taxes.basis: Explicitly supports progressive taxation, though the dossier gives no detailed revenue mechanism.

climate.basis: Explicitly supports renewable energy.

reform.basis: Explicitly supports ranked-choice voting and campaign reform.

sources.ref: 2026 primary candidate listing

candidates.name: Andres Valleza
candidates.summary: A filed District 8 candidate for whom the reviewed dossier found no verifiable issue positions.
highlights: Identity was matched to the official candidate record and campaign contact [S1][S2]
Policy axes are omitted because the dossier found no verifiable issue position [S1][S2]

sources.ref: 2026 primary candidate listing

candidates.name: Bob Hagglund
candidates.summary: A District 8 challenger emphasizing lower costs, local jobs, producer-focused trade, and market-oriented clean-energy innovation.
highlights: Supports lowering costs and expanding local jobs [S2]
Supports trade policy favoring Washington producers [S2]
Backs clean-energy innovation while opposing punitive green-energy taxes [S2]

climate.basis: Supports clean-energy innovation but prioritizes affordability and opposes punitive green-energy taxes.

sources.ref: 2026 primary candidate listing

contests.office_does: A state senator writes and votes on state laws and budgets, conducts oversight, and represents their legislative district.
contests.race_blurb: A long-serving senator emphasizing housing, infrastructure, and civil protections faces a former councilmember emphasizing lower taxes, spending restraint, public safety, and parental rights.

candidates.name: Marko Liias
candidates.summary: Liias is a long-serving state senator and transportation chair supporting housing and infrastructure investment, targeted tax relief, and LGBTQ and immigrant protections.
highlights: Supports affordable housing near transit, infrastructure, tax relief, and a working-family tax credit [S1]
Supports childcare and LGBTQ and immigrant protections [S1]
Reports securing housing resources and chairing transportation investment [S1]

taxes.basis: He supports targeted small-business tax relief and a working-family tax credit.

spending.basis: He supports and reports a record of housing and transportation investment.

housing.basis: He supports affordable housing near transit and reports securing housing resources.

experience.basis: He has served in the Senate since 2014 after service as a representative and councilmember.

immigration.basis: He explicitly supports protections for immigrants.

social.basis: He explicitly supports LGBTQ protections.

sources.ref: 2026 Washington primary voters' pamphlet candidate statement

candidates.name: Riaz Khan
candidates.summary: Khan is a former councilmember, labor representative, and business founder emphasizing lower taxes, spending restraint, public safety, and parental rights.

Supports public safety, affordable housing, education, and parental rights [S1]

taxes.basis: He explicitly supports lower taxes.

spending.basis: He explicitly supports spending restraint and transparency.

experience.basis: He combines former city-council service with private-sector and labor experience outside the Legislature.

social.basis: He explicitly supports parental rights.

sources.ref: 2026 Washington primary voters' pamphlet candidate statement

contests.office_does: A PUD commissioner sets policy for the customer-owned utility, including rates, reliability, resource planning, and utility services.
contests.race_blurb: Incumbent Sid Logan faces Bruce King and former Island County commissioner Janet St Clair in a race centered on rates, reliability, resource planning, and clean-energy investment.

candidates.name: Sid Logan
candidates.summary: Logan is the incumbent PUD commissioner, emphasizing rates, reliability, cybersecurity, renewable integration, and customer assistance.
highlights: Has served as PUD commissioner since 2017 and is an engineer and Energy Northwest director [S1]
Supports low rates, reliability, cyber preparation, renewable integration, and customer assistance [S1][S2]

sources.ref: Snohomish County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 62

candidates.name: Bruce King
candidates.summary: King is a technology entrepreneur and farmer emphasizing low-cost reliable service, broad resource options, and greater public input.
highlights: Reports decades of small-business experience [S1]
Supports planning across nuclear, solar, hydro, and fossil options with greater public input [S1][S2]

sources.ref: Snohomish County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 62

candidates.name: Janet St Clair
candidates.summary: St Clair is a former Island County commissioner emphasizing affordability, reliable service, and investment in water and energy resources.
highlights: Served eight years as Island County commissioner and previously led nonprofits [S1]
Promises low bills and reliable service alongside expanded water and energy investment [S1][S2]

sources.ref: Snohomish County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 63

contests.office_does: A U.S. representative writes and votes on federal law and spending, conducts executive oversight, and provides constituent services.
contests.race_blurb: Freshman incumbent Michael Baumgartner faces eleven challengers spanning independent reform, Democratic health-care and affordability platforms, and candidates with military, diplomatic, legal, medical, or business experience.

candidates.name: Nate Powell
candidates.summary: Powell is a firefighter and Marine veteran advocating billionaire-tax enforcement, universal health access, labor protections, waste reduction, and independence from corporate parties.
highlights: Supports ending billionaire tax avoidance and reducing waste and debt [S1][S2]
Supports universal health-care access and labor protections [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: He explicitly supports ending billionaire tax avoidance.

spending.basis: He combines universal-service ambitions with waste and debt reduction.

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate without an elected record.

reform.basis: He emphasizes independence from corporate parties.

healthcare.basis: He supports universal health-care access, though the dossier does not specify a single-payer mechanism.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Carmela Conroy
candidates.summary: Conroy is a former diplomat and deputy prosecutor emphasizing current-system health funding, reproductive autonomy, and executive and anti-corruption accountability.
highlights: Supports rural hospitals, Medicare, and Medicaid [S1][S2]
Supports reproductive autonomy and anti-corruption enforcement [S1][S2]

experience.basis: She has substantial diplomatic and prosecutorial experience but no documented elected record.

reform.basis: She emphasizes executive accountability and anti-corruption enforcement.

healthcare.basis: She supports funding rural hospitals, Medicare, and Medicaid within the current system.

social.basis: She explicitly supports reproductive autonomy.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Matthew Hayes
candidates.summary: Hayes is a retired physician and Air Force veteran emphasizing lower health costs, rural institutions, forests, and clean water.
highlights: Supports lower health-care costs and rural hospitals [S1][S2]
Supports farmers, schools, forests, and clean water [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate despite extensive military and medical experience.

healthcare.basis: He emphasizes lower costs and rural-hospital support without proposing single-payer transformation.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Bajun R. Mavalwalla
candidates.summary: Mavalwalla is a retired military officer and union member advocating balanced budgets, trade, alliances, infrastructure, and cross-party governance.
highlights: Supports balanced budgets and free trade [S1][S2]
Supports military alliances, infrastructure, and broadband [S1][S2]

spending.basis: He explicitly supports balanced budgets while also backing infrastructure investment.

experience.basis: He has military and campaign experience but no documented elected record.

defense.basis: He supports maintaining U.S. military alliances.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Michael McGarr
candidates.summary: McGarr is a veteran and retired editor advocating restored federal capacity, congressional oversight, and bipartisan institutional governance.
highlights: Supports restoring federal agencies and congressional oversight [S1][S2]
Opposes shutdowns and supports bipartisan governance [S1][S2]

spending.basis: He supports restoring federal-agency capacity rather than reducing it.

experience.basis: He offers military and journalism experience but no elected record.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Kevin Fagan
candidates.summary: Fagan is a filed Democratic candidate whose reviewed dossier documents only general affordability and representation priorities.
highlights: Official statement emphasizes affordability and representation [S1]

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Michael Baumgartner
candidates.summary: Baumgartner is the freshman incumbent and a former state senator and county treasurer emphasizing dams, agriculture, water, constituent service, and cross-party work.
highlights: Has served in Congress, the state Senate, and as county treasurer [S1][S2]
Supports Snake River dams, agriculture, water resources, and congressional integrity [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He is the incumbent with substantial prior state and county elected service.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Kyle Usrey
candidates.summary: Usrey is a lawyer and former university president advocating single-payer major-medical coverage, limits on ICE misconduct, AI regulation, and institutional term limits.
highlights: Supports single-payer major-medical reform [S1][S2]
Supports civil liability for unlawful ICE conduct, AI regulation, and Supreme Court term limits [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He has extensive professional and institutional leadership but no documented elected record.

reform.basis: He supports executive limits and Supreme Court term limits.

immigration.basis: He supports civil liability for unlawful ICE conduct.

healthcare.basis: He explicitly supports single-payer major-medical reform.

tech.basis: He explicitly supports AI regulation.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Andrew Bartleson
candidates.summary: Bartleson is a first-time candidate advocating universal health and dental coverage, antitrust action, political disclosure, and AI controls.
highlights: Supports universal health and dental coverage [S1][S2]
Supports stock-trading disclosure, antitrust action, and AI privacy and price-fixing controls [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate without an elected record.

reform.basis: He supports stock-trading disclosure as a political-accountability reform.

tech.basis: He supports AI privacy and price-fixing controls.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Ann Marie Danimus
candidates.summary: Danimus is an independent small-business consultant emphasizing no PAC money, affordability, reproductive rights, balanced immigration policy, and addiction services.
highlights: Rejects PAC money and supports program restructuring [S1][S2]
Supports abortion rights and immigration with border protection [S1][S2]

experience.basis: She offers business and caregiving experience rather than elected office.

reform.basis: She explicitly rejects PAC money and runs independently.

immigration.basis: She combines support for immigration with border protection.

social.basis: She explicitly supports abortion rights.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Richard Freudenberg
candidates.summary: Freudenberg is a retired Air Force lawyer emphasizing health access, rural hospitals, affordability, legal accountability, and opposition to tax cuts concentrated on wealthy people.
highlights: Supports health-care access and rural hospitals [S1][S2]
Opposes tax cuts concentrated on millionaires and billionaires [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: He opposes tax cuts concentrated on millionaires and billionaires.

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate despite extensive military legal experience.

healthcare.basis: He supports access and rural hospitals without proposing single-payer transformation.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: David Womack
candidates.summary: Womack is a retired military medical commander and rural-hospital executive advocating universal coverage, reproductive autonomy, climate and water research, and humane border security.
highlights: Supports universal coverage and sustainable rural hospitals [S1][S2]
Supports reproductive autonomy, climate research, water stewardship, and humane border security [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He has senior military and hospital experience but no elected record.

immigration.basis: His humane-border-security position combines enforcement and protections.

social.basis: He explicitly supports reproductive autonomy.

defense.basis: He supports defense while opposing forever wars.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

contests.office_does: The auditor administers elections and voter registration, records property documents, licenses vehicles and marriages, and performs county financial and payroll functions.
contests.race_blurb: In this open race, Callie Gee and Dale Whitaker foreground accounting credentials, while Michael Cathcart emphasizes elected management, oversight, and open-government work.

candidates.name: Callie Gee
candidates.summary: A public accountant, Gee emphasizes election access, vote-by-mail protection, transparent reporting, and private-sector financial management.
highlights: Works as a public accountant and has accounting and finance degrees [S1][S3]
Supports vote-by-mail protection, broad participation, and independent election oversight [S1][S2]
Promises transparent and fiscally responsible administration [S1]

spending.basis: She centers financial reporting, compliance, transparency, and fiscal responsibility.

experience.basis: She has professional accounting experience but no prior county-office management record.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 29

candidates.name: Dale Whitaker
candidates.summary: An enrolled agent and tax-practice owner, Whitaker presents the auditor primarily as an accounting and fraud-detection office.
highlights: Reports 18 years of accounting experience and prior corporate CFO work [S1][S3]
Proposes readable statements, modernized services, and close stewardship of public funds [S1][S2]
Emphasizes election security rather than an access-oriented program [S1]

spending.basis: His platform centers accounting controls, fraud detection, and close stewardship of existing funds.

experience.basis: He has extensive accounting and CFO experience but no county auditor management record.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 29

candidates.name: Michael Cathcart
candidates.summary: A Spokane city councilmember, Cathcart emphasizes his elected budget oversight, regional-board service, fraud prevention, and open-government record.
highlights: Has served on the Spokane City Council since 2020 with finance and regional-board roles [S1][S3]
Promises financial scrutiny, fraud prevention, election integrity, and public access to facts [S1][S2]
Points to budget oversight and open-government work as his relevant record [S1]

spending.basis: He repeatedly frames the office around scrutiny, fraud prevention, oversight, and public accountability.

experience.basis: He has the field's strongest elected and intergovernmental management record, though not direct auditor service.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 29

contests.office_does: The clerk maintains Superior Court records, receives filings and fees, supports courtroom administration, and manages lawful public access to records.
contests.race_blurb: Dave Lucas emphasizes court-administration experience and continuity; Elliot Robison emphasizes technology, accessibility, workplace relations, and operational reporting.

candidates.name: Elliot Robison
candidates.summary: A business-intelligence analyst, Robison proposes technology-driven workflow improvements and expanded public access tools.
highlights: Proposes expanded guides, notifications, translated forms, and public operational reporting [S1][S2]
Would apply capacity analysis and supports a union-friendly workplace [S1]

experience.basis: He offers relevant analytics and software experience but no direct court-administration record.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 30

candidates.name: Dave Lucas
candidates.summary: Cheney's municipal court administrator, Lucas presents direct court-management and military leadership experience as preparation for the clerk's office.
highlights: Serves as Cheney Municipal Court administrator and completed the state Court Administrators Academy [S1]
Proposes continued modernization with attention to speed, accuracy, and confidential information [S1]

experience.basis: He has direct court-administration experience, though not prior elected county-clerk service.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 30

contests.office_does: The prosecutor leads felony and juvenile prosecution, represents the county in civil matters, advises county government, and sets charging and diversion priorities within state law.
contests.race_blurb: Both candidates are deputy prosecutors. Danny Tarkenton emphasizes psychology-informed recidivism reduction and political independence, while incumbent Preston McCollam emphasizes enforcement, victim advocacy, and institutional continuity.

candidates.name: Danny Tarkenton
candidates.summary: A deputy prosecutor and Iraq War veteran, Tarkenton emphasizes behavioral evidence, recidivism reduction, and independence from party organizations.
highlights: Handles criminal and involuntary-treatment proceedings and reports a docket exceeding 500 cases [S1]
Says psychology-informed prosecution can reduce repeat offending [S1][S2]
Frames independence from political parties as important to prosecutorial discretion [S1]

safety.basis: Her public-safety approach emphasizes reducing recidivism through psychology-informed prosecution.

experience.basis: She has substantial current prosecutorial responsibility but not the incumbent's senior management record.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 31

candidates.name: Preston McCollam
candidates.summary: The incumbent prosecutor, McCollam emphasizes conventional enforcement, crime-victim advocacy, and continuity in senior office management.
highlights: Has served as county prosecutor since July 2025 after more than a decade in criminal and civil prosecution [S1]
Promises law-enforcement collaboration, offender accountability, and victim advocacy [S1][S2]
Previously served as chief criminal deputy and interim civil chief [S1]

safety.basis: He repeatedly centers law-enforcement collaboration, offender accountability, and conventional prosecution.

experience.basis: He is the incumbent with more than a decade of prosecution and senior office-management experience.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 31

contests.office_does: A U.S. representative writes and votes on federal law and spending, conducts executive oversight, and provides constituent services.
contests.race_blurb: Freshman incumbent Michael Baumgartner faces eleven challengers spanning independent reform, Democratic health-care and affordability platforms, and candidates with military, diplomatic, legal, medical, or business experience.

candidates.name: Nate Powell
candidates.summary: Powell is a firefighter and Marine veteran advocating billionaire-tax enforcement, universal health access, labor protections, waste reduction, and independence from corporate parties.
highlights: Supports ending billionaire tax avoidance and reducing waste and debt [S1][S2]
Supports universal health-care access and labor protections [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: He explicitly supports ending billionaire tax avoidance.

spending.basis: He combines universal-service ambitions with waste and debt reduction.

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate without an elected record.

reform.basis: He emphasizes independence from corporate parties.

healthcare.basis: He supports universal health-care access, though the dossier does not specify a single-payer mechanism.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Carmela Conroy
candidates.summary: Conroy is a former diplomat and deputy prosecutor emphasizing current-system health funding, reproductive autonomy, and executive and anti-corruption accountability.
highlights: Supports rural hospitals, Medicare, and Medicaid [S1][S2]
Supports reproductive autonomy and anti-corruption enforcement [S1][S2]

experience.basis: She has substantial diplomatic and prosecutorial experience but no documented elected record.

reform.basis: She emphasizes executive accountability and anti-corruption enforcement.

healthcare.basis: She supports funding rural hospitals, Medicare, and Medicaid within the current system.

social.basis: She explicitly supports reproductive autonomy.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Matthew Hayes
candidates.summary: Hayes is a retired physician and Air Force veteran emphasizing lower health costs, rural institutions, forests, and clean water.
highlights: Supports lower health-care costs and rural hospitals [S1][S2]
Supports farmers, schools, forests, and clean water [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate despite extensive military and medical experience.

healthcare.basis: He emphasizes lower costs and rural-hospital support without proposing single-payer transformation.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Bajun R. Mavalwalla
candidates.summary: Mavalwalla is a retired military officer and union member advocating balanced budgets, trade, alliances, infrastructure, and cross-party governance.
highlights: Supports balanced budgets and free trade [S1][S2]
Supports military alliances, infrastructure, and broadband [S1][S2]

spending.basis: He explicitly supports balanced budgets while also backing infrastructure investment.

experience.basis: He has military and campaign experience but no documented elected record.

defense.basis: He supports maintaining U.S. military alliances.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Michael McGarr
candidates.summary: McGarr is a veteran and retired editor advocating restored federal capacity, congressional oversight, and bipartisan institutional governance.
highlights: Supports restoring federal agencies and congressional oversight [S1][S2]
Opposes shutdowns and supports bipartisan governance [S1][S2]

spending.basis: He supports restoring federal-agency capacity rather than reducing it.

experience.basis: He offers military and journalism experience but no elected record.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Kevin Fagan
candidates.summary: Fagan is a filed Democratic candidate whose reviewed dossier documents only general affordability and representation priorities.
highlights: Official statement emphasizes affordability and representation [S1]

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Michael Baumgartner
candidates.summary: Baumgartner is the freshman incumbent and a former state senator and county treasurer emphasizing dams, agriculture, water, constituent service, and cross-party work.
highlights: Has served in Congress, the state Senate, and as county treasurer [S1][S2]
Supports Snake River dams, agriculture, water resources, and congressional integrity [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He is the incumbent with substantial prior state and county elected service.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Kyle Usrey
candidates.summary: Usrey is a lawyer and former university president advocating single-payer major-medical coverage, limits on ICE misconduct, AI regulation, and institutional term limits.
highlights: Supports single-payer major-medical reform [S1][S2]
Supports civil liability for unlawful ICE conduct, AI regulation, and Supreme Court term limits [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He has extensive professional and institutional leadership but no documented elected record.

reform.basis: He supports executive limits and Supreme Court term limits.

immigration.basis: He supports civil liability for unlawful ICE conduct.

healthcare.basis: He explicitly supports single-payer major-medical reform.

tech.basis: He explicitly supports AI regulation.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Andrew Bartleson
candidates.summary: Bartleson is a first-time candidate advocating universal health and dental coverage, antitrust action, political disclosure, and AI controls.
highlights: Supports universal health and dental coverage [S1][S2]
Supports stock-trading disclosure, antitrust action, and AI privacy and price-fixing controls [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate without an elected record.

reform.basis: He supports stock-trading disclosure as a political-accountability reform.

tech.basis: He supports AI privacy and price-fixing controls.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Ann Marie Danimus
candidates.summary: Danimus is an independent small-business consultant emphasizing no PAC money, affordability, reproductive rights, balanced immigration policy, and addiction services.
highlights: Rejects PAC money and supports program restructuring [S1][S2]
Supports abortion rights and immigration with border protection [S1][S2]

experience.basis: She offers business and caregiving experience rather than elected office.

reform.basis: She explicitly rejects PAC money and runs independently.

immigration.basis: She combines support for immigration with border protection.

social.basis: She explicitly supports abortion rights.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Richard Freudenberg
candidates.summary: Freudenberg is a retired Air Force lawyer emphasizing health access, rural hospitals, affordability, legal accountability, and opposition to tax cuts concentrated on wealthy people.
highlights: Supports health-care access and rural hospitals [S1][S2]
Opposes tax cuts concentrated on millionaires and billionaires [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: He opposes tax cuts concentrated on millionaires and billionaires.

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate despite extensive military legal experience.

healthcare.basis: He supports access and rural hospitals without proposing single-payer transformation.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: David Womack
candidates.summary: Womack is a retired military medical commander and rural-hospital executive advocating universal coverage, reproductive autonomy, climate and water research, and humane border security.
highlights: Supports universal coverage and sustainable rural hospitals [S1][S2]
Supports reproductive autonomy, climate research, water stewardship, and humane border security [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He has senior military and hospital experience but no elected record.

immigration.basis: His humane-border-security position combines enforcement and protections.

social.basis: He explicitly supports reproductive autonomy.

defense.basis: He supports defense while opposing forever wars.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

contests.office_does: A U.S. representative writes and votes on federal law and spending, conducts oversight, and provides constituent services.
contests.race_blurb: Two-term incumbent Marie Gluesenkamp Perez faces eight challengers spanning institutional conservatism, progressive economics, civil liberties, anti-party reform, and business-focused platforms.

candidates.name: Marie Gluesenkamp Perez
candidates.summary: Two-term incumbent and auto-shop owner emphasizing trades, manufacturing, rural investment, supply-side housing, abortion rights, and anti-corporate reforms.
highlights: Supports cutting red tape to build housing [S1]
Opposes corporate PAC money and introduced a congressional stock-trading ban [S1]
Supports abortion rights and rural health and infrastructure [S1]

spending.basis: Supports rural health, infrastructure, school, road, forest, and farmland investment.

reform.basis: Rejects corporate PAC money and introduced stock-trading and congressional-pay legislation.

healthcare.basis: Supports cheaper generics and rural care within existing programs.

social.basis: Explicit abortion-rights support.

experience.basis: Two-term incumbent with committee and legislative record.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 12

candidates.name: Brent Hennrich
candidates.summary: Project manager supporting progressive taxes, health-care expansion, infrastructure and social investment, ICE constraints, reduced war spending, and PAC independence.
highlights: Supports higher taxes on wealthy people and rejects corporate PAC money [S1]
Supports universal-health movement and investment [S1][S2]
Opposes wars and ICE practices [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicit higher taxes on wealthy people.

spending.basis: Broad school, infrastructure, health, broadband, and social investment.

reform.basis: Rejects corporate PAC funding.

immigration.basis: Opposes documented ICE practices.

healthcare.basis: Supports movement toward universal care but mechanism is unspecified.

defense.basis: Explicit opposition to wars.

experience.basis: No elected record.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 12

candidates.name: John P. Roco
candidates.summary: Therapist and former professor emphasizing immigration enforcement, traditional social positions, and a public-works housing concept.
highlights: Supports removing immigrants he describes as criminals [S1]
Opposes DEI in the military and transgender women in women's sports [S1]

immigration.basis: Supports removal of immigrants characterized as criminals while retaining others.

social.basis: Explicit traditional positions on military DEI and transgender sports.

experience.basis: No elected federal record.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 13

candidates.name: John Saulie-Rohman
candidates.summary: Power-industry operator centered on anti-donor, anti-two-party, anti-intervention, fiscal, and corporate-power critiques.
highlights: Argues major parties serve donors and corporations [S1][S2]
Criticizes military intervention and national-debt growth [S1]

spending.basis: Criticizes national debt growth.

reform.basis: Structural anti-donor and anti-two-party platform.

defense.basis: Opposes military intervention.

experience.basis: No elected experience.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 13

candidates.name: Troy Rasband
candidates.summary: IT professional supporting a wealth tax, expanded nutrition aid, low-income advocacy, and more affordable health care.
highlights: Supports a wealth tax and expanded SNAP [S1][S2]
Supports low-income advocacy and more affordable health care [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicit wealth-tax support.

spending.basis: Supports expanded SNAP and low-income programs.

experience.basis: No elected record.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 14

candidates.name: John Braun
candidates.summary: State Senate Republican leader and retired Navy captain emphasizing tax and spending restraint, enforcement, defense, border security, and parental rights.
highlights: Supports lower taxes, reduced spending, law enforcement, and parental rights [S1]
Supports border security and national defense [S3]
Long state legislative and budget record [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: Explicit lower-tax platform.

spending.basis: Explicit reduced spending and waste-control platform.

safety.basis: Explicit law-enforcement priority.

immigration.basis: Explicit border-security priority.

social.basis: Supports parental rights.

defense.basis: National-defense support and retired Navy leadership.

experience.basis: Long legislative, budget, military, and business leadership record.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 14

candidates.name: Antony Barran
candidates.summary: Entrepreneur and oyster farmer emphasizing business, workforce, affordable energy, education, natural resources, and outsider pragmatism.
highlights: Supports local economies, workforce training, small businesses, and affordable energy [S1][S2]
Runs against political insiders [S1]

climate.basis: Prioritizes energy affordability with responsible-resource language.

experience.basis: Business outsider with no elected record beyond precinct service.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 15

candidates.name: Austin Braswell
candidates.summary: Navy veteran emphasizing civil liberties, police and ICE accountability, surveillance limits, PAC independence, lower war spending, and AI ownership protections.
highlights: Supports police accountability and keeping ICE out of Vancouver [S1][S2]
Rejects corporate PAC money and supports reducing war spending [S1]
Supports surveillance limits and AI ownership protections [S1]

safety.basis: Police-accountability emphasis leans away from enforcement-first.

reform.basis: Rejects corporate PAC money and supports federal transparency.

immigration.basis: Explicitly supports excluding ICE from Vancouver.

tech.basis: Supports protections against AI-related ownership loss and surveillance.

defense.basis: Explicitly supports reducing war spending.

experience.basis: No elected record.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 15

candidates.name: Lawrence Kellogg
candidates.summary: Retired pilot supporting lower taxes, border enforcement, business conditions, transparency, voter access, and rejection of unsupported fraud claims.
highlights: Criticizes high taxation and open borders [S1]
Supports voter access and congressional transparency [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicit criticism of high taxation.

immigration.basis: Explicit open-border criticism and enforcement posture.

reform.basis: Supports transparency and voter access while rejecting unsupported fraud claims.

experience.basis: No elected record.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 16

contests.office_does: County commissioners adopt the county budget and laws and oversee land use, public health, public safety, infrastructure, and county services.
contests.race_blurb: Incumbent Emily Clouse emphasizes housing and climate investments, Lacey Councilmember Nicolas Martinez-Dunning emphasizes fiscal restraint and reform, and Michelle Gipson emphasizes housing, safety, families, and managed growth.

candidates.name: Nicolas Martinez-Dunning
candidates.summary: Martinez-Dunning is a Lacey councilmember campaigning on deficits, transparency, cross-party management, and independence from PAC funding.
highlights: Serves on the Lacey City Council and has business, nonprofit, and military experience [S1]
Highlights deficits and trust concerns and rejects PAC and special-interest funding [S1][S2]

spending.basis: He centers county deficits, fiscal management, and accountability.

experience.basis: He has current elected municipal experience plus business and nonprofit management.

reform.basis: He rejects PAC and special-interest funding and emphasizes transparency and trust reform.

candidates.name: Emily Clouse
candidates.summary: Clouse is the incumbent commissioner, citing housing, supportive-service, climate, and restoration investments alongside process-accountability concerns.

Cites heat-pump discounts and estuary restoration [S1][S2]
Opposed a budget process she said lacked public input [S1][S2]

spending.basis: Her record centers substantial public investment in housing, supportive units, and climate programs.

housing.basis: She cites direct county funding for affordable housing and supportive units.

climate.basis: She cites heat-pump discounts and estuary restoration as core record items.

experience.basis: She is the incumbent with an established county governing record.

candidates.name: Michelle Gipson

Supports affordable housing, community safety, youth and families, environmental protection, and responsible growth [S1][S2]

housing.basis: She explicitly supports affordable housing, though the dossier does not specify the policy mechanism.

experience.basis: She has elected school-governance and nonprofit operations experience.

sources.ref: Thurston County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 49

contests.office_does: A U.S. representative writes and votes on federal law and spending, conducts oversight, and provides constituent services.
contests.race_blurb: Two-term incumbent Marie Gluesenkamp Perez faces eight challengers spanning institutional conservatism, progressive economics, civil liberties, anti-party reform, and business-focused platforms.

candidates.name: Marie Gluesenkamp Perez
candidates.summary: Two-term incumbent and auto-shop owner emphasizing trades, manufacturing, rural investment, supply-side housing, abortion rights, and anti-corporate reforms.
highlights: Supports cutting red tape to build housing [S1]
Opposes corporate PAC money and introduced a congressional stock-trading ban [S1]
Supports abortion rights and rural health and infrastructure [S1]

spending.basis: Supports rural health, infrastructure, school, road, forest, and farmland investment.

reform.basis: Rejects corporate PAC money and introduced stock-trading and congressional-pay legislation.

healthcare.basis: Supports cheaper generics and rural care within existing programs.

social.basis: Explicit abortion-rights support.

experience.basis: Two-term incumbent with committee and legislative record.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 12

candidates.name: Brent Hennrich
candidates.summary: Project manager supporting progressive taxes, health-care expansion, infrastructure and social investment, ICE constraints, reduced war spending, and PAC independence.
highlights: Supports higher taxes on wealthy people and rejects corporate PAC money [S1]
Supports universal-health movement and investment [S1][S2]
Opposes wars and ICE practices [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicit higher taxes on wealthy people.

spending.basis: Broad school, infrastructure, health, broadband, and social investment.

reform.basis: Rejects corporate PAC funding.

immigration.basis: Opposes documented ICE practices.

healthcare.basis: Supports movement toward universal care but mechanism is unspecified.

defense.basis: Explicit opposition to wars.

experience.basis: No elected record.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 12

candidates.name: John P. Roco
candidates.summary: Therapist and former professor emphasizing immigration enforcement, traditional social positions, and a public-works housing concept.
highlights: Supports removing immigrants he describes as criminals [S1]
Opposes DEI in the military and transgender women in women's sports [S1]

immigration.basis: Supports removal of immigrants characterized as criminals while retaining others.

social.basis: Explicit traditional positions on military DEI and transgender sports.

experience.basis: No elected federal record.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 13

candidates.name: John Saulie-Rohman
candidates.summary: Power-industry operator centered on anti-donor, anti-two-party, anti-intervention, fiscal, and corporate-power critiques.
highlights: Argues major parties serve donors and corporations [S1][S2]
Criticizes military intervention and national-debt growth [S1]

spending.basis: Criticizes national debt growth.

reform.basis: Structural anti-donor and anti-two-party platform.

defense.basis: Opposes military intervention.

experience.basis: No elected experience.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 13

candidates.name: Troy Rasband
candidates.summary: IT professional supporting a wealth tax, expanded nutrition aid, low-income advocacy, and more affordable health care.
highlights: Supports a wealth tax and expanded SNAP [S1][S2]
Supports low-income advocacy and more affordable health care [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicit wealth-tax support.

spending.basis: Supports expanded SNAP and low-income programs.

experience.basis: No elected record.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 14

candidates.name: John Braun
candidates.summary: State Senate Republican leader and retired Navy captain emphasizing tax and spending restraint, enforcement, defense, border security, and parental rights.
highlights: Supports lower taxes, reduced spending, law enforcement, and parental rights [S1]
Supports border security and national defense [S3]
Long state legislative and budget record [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: Explicit lower-tax platform.

spending.basis: Explicit reduced spending and waste-control platform.

safety.basis: Explicit law-enforcement priority.

immigration.basis: Explicit border-security priority.

social.basis: Supports parental rights.

defense.basis: National-defense support and retired Navy leadership.

experience.basis: Long legislative, budget, military, and business leadership record.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 14

candidates.name: Antony Barran
candidates.summary: Entrepreneur and oyster farmer emphasizing business, workforce, affordable energy, education, natural resources, and outsider pragmatism.
highlights: Supports local economies, workforce training, small businesses, and affordable energy [S1][S2]
Runs against political insiders [S1]

climate.basis: Prioritizes energy affordability with responsible-resource language.

experience.basis: Business outsider with no elected record beyond precinct service.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 15

candidates.name: Austin Braswell
candidates.summary: Navy veteran emphasizing civil liberties, police and ICE accountability, surveillance limits, PAC independence, lower war spending, and AI ownership protections.
highlights: Supports police accountability and keeping ICE out of Vancouver [S1][S2]
Rejects corporate PAC money and supports reducing war spending [S1]
Supports surveillance limits and AI ownership protections [S1]

safety.basis: Police-accountability emphasis leans away from enforcement-first.

reform.basis: Rejects corporate PAC money and supports federal transparency.

immigration.basis: Explicitly supports excluding ICE from Vancouver.

tech.basis: Supports protections against AI-related ownership loss and surveillance.

defense.basis: Explicitly supports reducing war spending.

experience.basis: No elected record.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 15

candidates.name: Lawrence Kellogg
candidates.summary: Retired pilot supporting lower taxes, border enforcement, business conditions, transparency, voter access, and rejection of unsupported fraud claims.
highlights: Criticizes high taxation and open borders [S1]
Supports voter access and congressional transparency [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicit criticism of high taxation.

immigration.basis: Explicit open-border criticism and enforcement posture.

reform.basis: Supports transparency and voter access while rejecting unsupported fraud claims.

experience.basis: No elected record.

sources.ref: Clark County 2026 Primary Voters' Pamphlet page 16

contests.race_blurb: Freshman incumbent Michael Baumgartner faces eleven challengers spanning independent reform, Democratic health-care and affordability platforms, and candidates with military, diplomatic, legal, medical, or business experience.

candidates.name: Nate Powell
candidates.summary: Powell is a firefighter and Marine veteran advocating billionaire-tax enforcement, universal health access, labor protections, waste reduction, and independence from corporate parties.
highlights: Supports ending billionaire tax avoidance and reducing waste and debt [S1][S2]
Supports universal health-care access and labor protections [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: He explicitly supports ending billionaire tax avoidance.

spending.basis: He combines universal-service ambitions with waste and debt reduction.

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate without an elected record.

reform.basis: He emphasizes independence from corporate parties.

healthcare.basis: He supports universal health-care access, though the dossier does not specify a single-payer mechanism.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Carmela Conroy
candidates.summary: Conroy is a former diplomat and deputy prosecutor emphasizing current-system health funding, reproductive autonomy, and executive and anti-corruption accountability.
highlights: Supports rural hospitals, Medicare, and Medicaid [S1][S2]
Supports reproductive autonomy and anti-corruption enforcement [S1][S2]

experience.basis: She has substantial diplomatic and prosecutorial experience but no documented elected record.

reform.basis: She emphasizes executive accountability and anti-corruption enforcement.

healthcare.basis: She supports funding rural hospitals, Medicare, and Medicaid within the current system.

social.basis: She explicitly supports reproductive autonomy.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Matthew Hayes
candidates.summary: Hayes is a retired physician and Air Force veteran emphasizing lower health costs, rural institutions, forests, and clean water.
highlights: Supports lower health-care costs and rural hospitals [S1][S2]
Supports farmers, schools, forests, and clean water [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate despite extensive military and medical experience.

healthcare.basis: He emphasizes lower costs and rural-hospital support without proposing single-payer transformation.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Bajun R. Mavalwalla
candidates.summary: Mavalwalla is a retired military officer and union member advocating balanced budgets, trade, alliances, infrastructure, and cross-party governance.
highlights: Supports balanced budgets and free trade [S1][S2]
Supports military alliances, infrastructure, and broadband [S1][S2]

spending.basis: He explicitly supports balanced budgets while also backing infrastructure investment.

experience.basis: He has military and campaign experience but no documented elected record.

defense.basis: He supports maintaining U.S. military alliances.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Michael McGarr
candidates.summary: McGarr is a veteran and retired editor advocating restored federal capacity, congressional oversight, and bipartisan institutional governance.
highlights: Supports restoring federal agencies and congressional oversight [S1][S2]
Opposes shutdowns and supports bipartisan governance [S1][S2]

spending.basis: He supports restoring federal-agency capacity rather than reducing it.

experience.basis: He offers military and journalism experience but no elected record.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Kevin Fagan
candidates.summary: Fagan is a filed Democratic candidate whose reviewed dossier documents only general affordability and representation priorities.
highlights: Official statement emphasizes affordability and representation [S1]

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Michael Baumgartner
candidates.summary: Baumgartner is the freshman incumbent and a former state senator and county treasurer emphasizing dams, agriculture, water, constituent service, and cross-party work.
highlights: Has served in Congress, the state Senate, and as county treasurer [S1][S2]
Supports Snake River dams, agriculture, water resources, and congressional integrity [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He is the incumbent with substantial prior state and county elected service.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Kyle Usrey
candidates.summary: Usrey is a lawyer and former university president advocating single-payer major-medical coverage, limits on ICE misconduct, AI regulation, and institutional term limits.
highlights: Supports single-payer major-medical reform [S1][S2]
Supports civil liability for unlawful ICE conduct, AI regulation, and Supreme Court term limits [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He has extensive professional and institutional leadership but no documented elected record.

reform.basis: He supports executive limits and Supreme Court term limits.

immigration.basis: He supports civil liability for unlawful ICE conduct.

healthcare.basis: He explicitly supports single-payer major-medical reform.

tech.basis: He explicitly supports AI regulation.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Andrew Bartleson
candidates.summary: Bartleson is a first-time candidate advocating universal health and dental coverage, antitrust action, political disclosure, and AI controls.
highlights: Supports universal health and dental coverage [S1][S2]
Supports stock-trading disclosure, antitrust action, and AI privacy and price-fixing controls [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate without an elected record.

reform.basis: He supports stock-trading disclosure as a political-accountability reform.

tech.basis: He supports AI privacy and price-fixing controls.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Ann Marie Danimus
candidates.summary: Danimus is an independent small-business consultant emphasizing no PAC money, affordability, reproductive rights, balanced immigration policy, and addiction services.
highlights: Rejects PAC money and supports program restructuring [S1][S2]
Supports abortion rights and immigration with border protection [S1][S2]

experience.basis: She offers business and caregiving experience rather than elected office.

reform.basis: She explicitly rejects PAC money and runs independently.

immigration.basis: She combines support for immigration with border protection.

social.basis: She explicitly supports abortion rights.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Richard Freudenberg
candidates.summary: Freudenberg is a retired Air Force lawyer emphasizing health access, rural hospitals, affordability, legal accountability, and opposition to tax cuts concentrated on wealthy people.
highlights: Supports health-care access and rural hospitals [S1][S2]
Opposes tax cuts concentrated on millionaires and billionaires [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: He opposes tax cuts concentrated on millionaires and billionaires.

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate despite extensive military legal experience.

healthcare.basis: He supports access and rural hospitals without proposing single-payer transformation.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: David Womack
candidates.summary: Womack is a retired military medical commander and rural-hospital executive advocating universal coverage, reproductive autonomy, climate and water research, and humane border security.
highlights: Supports universal coverage and sustainable rural hospitals [S1][S2]
Supports reproductive autonomy, climate research, water stewardship, and humane border security [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He has senior military and hospital experience but no elected record.

immigration.basis: His humane-border-security position combines enforcement and protections.

social.basis: He explicitly supports reproductive autonomy.

defense.basis: He supports defense while opposing forever wars.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

contests.race_blurb: Long-serving incumbent Rick Larsen faces Edwin Feller's smaller-government campaign and two progressive challengers, Tomas Scheel and Devin Hermanson, who advocate more structural changes.

candidates.name: Edwin H. Feller
candidates.summary: Feller campaigns on smaller government, lower taxes, balanced budgets, border enforcement, law enforcement, and traditional Title IX protections.
highlights: Attributes affordability problems to taxes and government growth [S1]
Supports border security, law enforcement, election security, and balanced budgets [S1][S2]
Offers extensive private-sector work but no elected record [S1]

taxes.basis: He explicitly identifies high taxes and government growth as causes of affordability problems.

spending.basis: He emphasizes smaller government and balanced budgets.

safety.basis: He supports law enforcement as a central public-safety approach.

experience.basis: He offers private-sector and overseas facilities experience but no elected record.

immigration.basis: He explicitly prioritizes border security and enforcement.

social.basis: His Title IX position is framed around traditional sex-based protections for women.

sources.ref: Snohomish County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 16

candidates.name: Tomas Scheel
candidates.summary: Scheel combines universal health care and clean-energy investment with progressive social policy, replacing ICE, and restrictions on institutional campaign money.
highlights: Supports universal health care, clean energy, and major housing investment [S1][S2]
Calls for dismantling ICE and replacing it with a rights-grounded system [S1]
Rejects corporate PAC and foreign-lobby money [S1]

spending.basis: He supports major federal investment in health care, housing, and clean energy.

climate.basis: He explicitly supports a clean-energy transition.

experience.basis: He is an outsider candidate whose experience is military, technical, business, and volunteer service rather than elected office.

reform.basis: He rejects corporate PAC and foreign-lobby money and calls for higher official-accountability standards.

immigration.basis: He calls for dismantling ICE and replacing it with a rights-grounded immigration system.

healthcare.basis: He explicitly supports universal health care.

social.basis: He supports reproductive freedom and access to gender-affirming care.

sources.ref: Snohomish County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 17

candidates.name: Devin Hermanson
candidates.summary: Hermanson runs to the incumbent's left on wealth taxation, universal health care, climate investment, institutional reform, and U.S. aid to Israel.
highlights: Supports taxing billionaires, Medicare for All, and the Green New Deal [S1][S2]
Rejects corporate money and supports Supreme Court reform [S1][S2]
Calls for ending U.S. aid to Israel [S1]

taxes.basis: He explicitly supports taxing billionaires to fund a broader public agenda.

spending.basis: The Green New Deal and Medicare for All place expanded public investment at the center of his platform.

climate.basis: He explicitly supports the Green New Deal.

experience.basis: He is a challenger without elected office who frames his campaign around institutional rebuilding.

reform.basis: He rejects corporate money and calls for structural Supreme Court reform.

healthcare.basis: He explicitly supports Medicare for All.

defense.basis: He calls for ending U.S. aid to Israel.

sources.ref: Snohomish County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 18

candidates.name: Rick Larsen
candidates.summary: The long-serving incumbent emphasizes incremental expansion of health-care access, housing and infrastructure investment, fentanyl enforcement, and legal constraints on ICE.
highlights: Supports expanding health-care access and lowering costs [S1][S2]
Supports affordable-housing supply, infrastructure, jobs, and childcare [S1]
Supports fentanyl enforcement while requiring ICE agents to follow ordinary law-enforcement rules [S1]

spending.basis: He supports federal investment in housing, infrastructure, jobs, and childcare.

safety.basis: He combines law-enforcement action against fentanyl with constraints on immigration agents.

experience.basis: He is a long-serving incumbent with an extensive congressional record.

immigration.basis: He opposes executive actions targeting immigrant communities and supports law-enforcement rules for ICE.

healthcare.basis: He proposes expanding access and lowering costs within the current health-care system.

sources.ref: Snohomish County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet page 19

candidates.name: Jesse Corkern

Proposes consistent charging, training, supervision, reduced delays, and balanced caseloads [S2][S3]

safety.basis: He emphasizes consistent charging and law-enforcement partnerships alongside fairness and management reform.

experience.basis: He has direct senior prosecutor-office, defense, and civil-litigation experience.

contests.race_blurb: Freshman incumbent Michael Baumgartner faces eleven challengers spanning independent reform, Democratic health-care and affordability platforms, and candidates with military, diplomatic, legal, medical, or business experience.

candidates.name: Nate Powell
candidates.summary: Powell is a firefighter and Marine veteran advocating billionaire-tax enforcement, universal health access, labor protections, waste reduction, and independence from corporate parties.
highlights: Supports ending billionaire tax avoidance and reducing waste and debt [S1][S2]
Supports universal health-care access and labor protections [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: He explicitly supports ending billionaire tax avoidance.

spending.basis: He combines universal-service ambitions with waste and debt reduction.

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate without an elected record.

reform.basis: He emphasizes independence from corporate parties.

healthcare.basis: He supports universal health-care access, though the dossier does not specify a single-payer mechanism.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Carmela Conroy
candidates.summary: Conroy is a former diplomat and deputy prosecutor emphasizing current-system health funding, reproductive autonomy, and executive and anti-corruption accountability.
highlights: Supports rural hospitals, Medicare, and Medicaid [S1][S2]
Supports reproductive autonomy and anti-corruption enforcement [S1][S2]

experience.basis: She has substantial diplomatic and prosecutorial experience but no documented elected record.

reform.basis: She emphasizes executive accountability and anti-corruption enforcement.

healthcare.basis: She supports funding rural hospitals, Medicare, and Medicaid within the current system.

social.basis: She explicitly supports reproductive autonomy.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Matthew Hayes
candidates.summary: Hayes is a retired physician and Air Force veteran emphasizing lower health costs, rural institutions, forests, and clean water.
highlights: Supports lower health-care costs and rural hospitals [S1][S2]
Supports farmers, schools, forests, and clean water [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate despite extensive military and medical experience.

healthcare.basis: He emphasizes lower costs and rural-hospital support without proposing single-payer transformation.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Bajun R. Mavalwalla
candidates.summary: Mavalwalla is a retired military officer and union member advocating balanced budgets, trade, alliances, infrastructure, and cross-party governance.
highlights: Supports balanced budgets and free trade [S1][S2]
Supports military alliances, infrastructure, and broadband [S1][S2]

spending.basis: He explicitly supports balanced budgets while also backing infrastructure investment.

experience.basis: He has military and campaign experience but no documented elected record.

defense.basis: He supports maintaining U.S. military alliances.

candidates.name: Michael McGarr
candidates.summary: McGarr is a veteran and retired editor advocating restored federal capacity, congressional oversight, and bipartisan institutional governance.
highlights: Supports restoring federal agencies and congressional oversight [S1][S2]
Opposes shutdowns and supports bipartisan governance [S1][S2]

spending.basis: He supports restoring federal-agency capacity rather than reducing it.

experience.basis: He offers military and journalism experience but no elected record.

candidates.name: Michael Baumgartner
candidates.summary: Baumgartner is the freshman incumbent and a former state senator and county treasurer emphasizing dams, agriculture, water, constituent service, and cross-party work.
highlights: Has served in Congress, the state Senate, and as county treasurer [S1][S2]
Supports Snake River dams, agriculture, water resources, and congressional integrity [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He is the incumbent with substantial prior state and county elected service.

candidates.name: Kyle Usrey
candidates.summary: Usrey is a lawyer and former university president advocating single-payer major-medical coverage, limits on ICE misconduct, AI regulation, and institutional term limits.
highlights: Supports single-payer major-medical reform [S1][S2]
Supports civil liability for unlawful ICE conduct, AI regulation, and Supreme Court term limits [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He has extensive professional and institutional leadership but no documented elected record.

reform.basis: He supports executive limits and Supreme Court term limits.

immigration.basis: He supports civil liability for unlawful ICE conduct.

healthcare.basis: He explicitly supports single-payer major-medical reform.

tech.basis: He explicitly supports AI regulation.

candidates.name: Andrew Bartleson

highlights: Supports universal health and dental coverage [S1][S2]
Supports stock-trading disclosure, antitrust action, and AI privacy and price-fixing controls [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate without an elected record.

reform.basis: He supports stock-trading disclosure as a political-accountability reform.

tech.basis: He supports AI privacy and price-fixing controls.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: Ann Marie Danimus
candidates.summary: Danimus is an independent small-business consultant emphasizing no PAC money, affordability, reproductive rights, balanced immigration policy, and addiction services.
highlights: Rejects PAC money and supports program restructuring [S1][S2]
Supports abortion rights and immigration with border protection [S1][S2]

experience.basis: She offers business and caregiving experience rather than elected office.

reform.basis: She explicitly rejects PAC money and runs independently.

immigration.basis: She combines support for immigration with border protection.

social.basis: She explicitly supports abortion rights.

Opposes tax cuts concentrated on millionaires and billionaires [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: He opposes tax cuts concentrated on millionaires and billionaires.

experience.basis: He is a first-time candidate despite extensive military legal experience.

healthcare.basis: He supports access and rural hospitals without proposing single-payer transformation.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

candidates.name: David Womack
candidates.summary: Womack is a retired military medical commander and rural-hospital executive advocating universal coverage, reproductive autonomy, climate and water research, and humane border security.
highlights: Supports universal coverage and sustainable rural hospitals [S1][S2]
Supports reproductive autonomy, climate research, water stewardship, and humane border security [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He has senior military and hospital experience but no elected record.

immigration.basis: His humane-border-security position combines enforcement and protections.

social.basis: He explicitly supports reproductive autonomy.

defense.basis: He supports defense while opposing forever wars.

sources.ref: Spokane County 2026 primary voters' pamphlet pages 8-13

contests.office_does: The prosecutor brings criminal cases, advises county government, and represents the county in civil matters.
contests.race_blurb: Two attorneys differ most clearly on enforcement specificity and breadth of legal background.

candidates.name: Quinn Dalan
candidates.summary: Attorney with prosecution, defense, civil, and pro-tem judging experience emphasizing fairness, accountability, and trust.
highlights: Has worked on prosecution and defense sides [S2]
Platform is principle-oriented with limited operational detail [S2]

experience.basis: Broad legal and pro-tem judicial experience, though no elected prosecutor tenure.

candidates.name: Tyler Haueter
candidates.summary: Former special-assault prosecutor and current assistant city attorney emphasizing gangs, repeat violent offenders, victims, and law-enforcement coordination.
highlights: Prioritizes gang enforcement and repeat violent-offender prosecution [S2]
Calls for stronger victim services and law-enforcement coordination [S2]

safety.basis: Explicit enforcement-first agenda centered on gangs and repeat violent offenders.

experience.basis: Direct prosecutorial and municipal-law experience.

candidates.summary: Sheriff's lieutenant with 28 years in law enforcement emphasizing training, equipment, staffing, professional standards, enforcement, and prevention.

Combines enforcement against drugs and property crime with evidence-based prevention [S2]

safety.basis: Explicitly combines enforcement with evidence-based prevention.
